The Navy Secretary suspects the company's owner Philip Wickes is to blame, and asks NCIS to join forces with the PsyOps unit in an elaborate ruse to bring the culprit out of hiding. Meanwhile, Gibbs' colleagues begin to notice his attraction to his opposite number Samantha Ryan. Guest starring Jamie Lee Curtis.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Vance's brother-in-law Michael Thomas is linked to the murder of a petty officer, and Gibbs is placed under pressure to clear the man's name. Meanwhile, a negative performance review leaves Tony feeling downhearted and he resolves to prove the assessment wrong by being less chatty while on duty. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Rocky Carroll.(n)

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team travels to Ireland to mark one year since Megan's death, and Walter discovers a disaster at a nearby lake has created a poisonous cloud that could wipe out a village.(n)

programme.title: Scorpion

programme.desc: Part one of two. Reg organises a half-centenary celebration of Sun Hill, attended by dignitaries including former DCS Charles Barnett. programme.desc: Boycie uncovers disturbing information regarding his ancestral line. Tyler's surprising return from university adds to his sense of worry, convincing him something serious is about to happen. John Challis and Sue Holderness star.(n)

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: The trio scheme to get Wally out from under Nora Batty's thumb for a day, but come a cropper sneaking him back to the house. Comedy, starring Bill Owen and Joe Gladwin.(n)

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: The trio stumble across a health farm on a day out, leading Foggy to devise an ingenious money-making scam - selling food to its famished inhabitants. Comedy, starring Brian Wilde.(n)

programme.title: Inspector George Gently
programme.desc: A schoolgirl's killing brings Gently into the alien world of pop and media celebrity when it turns out the victim's best friend is a rising TV star. Bacchus suspects the dead girl's music teacher, since rumours persist that she was having an affair with him. But when it seems everyone has a different opinion of the girl, Gently must uncover these different faces to get to the truth of her murder. Neil Morrissey guest stars alongside Martin Shaw and Lee Ingleby.(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: While Brian spends time at home making peace with himself over his dismissal, he agrees to help Esther's friend Margaret find her missing brother Peter, in the interest of keeping his mind active. Peter was the accountant of a man who has gone to prison for the murder of his wife, so Brian visits the convict hoping he will be able to assist. However, his investigation begins to encroach on the Ucos team's efforts to track down a conman who may be connected to several outstanding cases. With Rosalind Ayres and Art Malik.(n)

Ian Kelsey and Robert Gwilym star.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. In an episode originally broadcast live, distraught dad Jeff Clarke holds the Sun Hill stalwarts at gunpoint, prompting a desperate response from Dan Casper - who ends up getting shot, along with the real Gabriel Kent. Fortunately, Terry Perkins is able to stay cool and assume the role of mediator, helping to defuse the situation while Acting Superintendent Amanda Prosser flounders helplessly.(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Nadia does not want to be in the dark anymore and invites Cathy for a drink! Richard wants to find out what the market holders are planning.(n)

programme.title: London's Burning
programme.desc: Billy confronts Lauren about her prostitution and Sicknote finds himself the unwitting accomplice in a jailbreak. Meanwhile, Skip helps a suicidal girl.(n)

programme.title: Monarch of the Glen
programme.desc: Hostile buyer Finlay makes an offer for Glenbogle, prompting the family to search for new homes. Paul tries to rally the crofters for a community buy-out - but discovers the would-be purchaser is intent on revenge. Iona prepares to marry Malcolm - who asks his love rival to give the bride away - Molly seeks advice from Hector's spirit, and Donald is freed from an old folks' home thanks to Ewan and Amy's daring escape plan.(n)

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: Dennis Waterman and former Corrie actress Georgia Taylor guest star in this special festive edition of the popular rural drama. A retired mountaineer (Waterman) still grieving over his son's death in a climbing accident gets a pleasant surprise when his student daughter (Taylor) returns for the holidays. But an even bigger shock is yet to come - not only is she pregnant, but the father-to-be is the very man her dad blames for her brother's demise. Meanwhile, the local women grow fed up with their menfolk when they arrive back from a booze run the worse for drink, prompting them to hang up their aprons and hand over all the Christmas organisation to the fellas. Beth doesn't know what to think when a patient predicts the new year will bring her and Tom the joy they have longed for, and romance is in the air for David and Anna. Lesley Dunlop, Philip Middlemiss, Kerrie Taylor and Samantha Giles star.(n)

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: Bryan welcomes his mail-order bride into his life - and his caravan - but there isn't enough room for her family as well. programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The unsolved murder of pornographer Jim Hockney is reopened when his daughter Sara provides DNA proof he wasn't her father. The team tries to track down jazz singer Angela Gold, who was the victim's girlfriend at the time and disappeared from the music scene after being acquitted of the crime, and it's Brian's replacement Danny Griffin (Nicholas Lyndhurst) who manages to find her. However, with Angela professing her innocence and Sara's mother Jane also arousing suspicion, it becomes a struggle to determine who is telling the truth. programme.desc: Tricia becomes emotional when her attempts to reunite a dying man with his drug-abusing son are too close to home, and Mark organises a rushed wedding with the ailing nurse. Matt struggles to understand why his patient will not respond to medication, questioning whether Donna administered it correctly, while Chrissie and Kyla argue over the running of the ward.(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Chloe finds herself short of cash and is subsequently labelled prime suspect when a patient discovers her purse has gone missing. Meanwhile, a distraught father learns his son has been attacked by football hooligans, Duffy returns from a romantic weekend with her husband and Mel's fear of dogs is exposed. Medical drama, starring Jan Anderson, Robert Gwilym and Cathy Shipton.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Adam Okaro returns to Sun Hill, where he resumes his role as Superintendent - and immediately asks Jo Masters to find out how the press obtained a photo of Lesley Palmer. Meanwhile, Smithy and Laura Bryant continue to investigate the real Gabriel Kent's identity - and try to fathom the true nature of his relationship with June Ackland.(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders

programme.title: Where the Heart Is

programme.desc: Foggy's experiments with camouflage only succeed in frightening innocent passers-by. Brian Wilde stars in Roy Clarke's long-running comedy.(n)

To test the sewers' handling skills, judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young kick off the pattern challenge with a boned basque. The task requires sewing together small pattern pieces for a precise fit and creating a tricky hook and eye fastening. Next, in the transformation challenge, the sewers transform pyjamas and nightwear into a summer dress, before finally being tasked to create a two-piece female sleep set which has to incorporates lace somewhere in their design.(n)

Laura's plan involves turning the property into a light-filled interconnected series of spaces, while Robert turns conventional wisdom on its head with a visually striking and flexible design. After experiencing both potential houses, the owners must choose which they would like to put into practice.(n)

programme.title: Letterbox
programme.desc: Game show hosted by Mel Giedroyc, as pairs of contestants go head-to-head to crack a series of passwords, in an attempt to take home £2,500 in cash. As the teams solve the passwords, they win letters that will help them later in the game, giving them a better chance of cracking the final password which unlocks the Letterbox holding the cash prize. However, if they end up as the losing pair along the way, they'll have to surrender all the letters they've won to the winning pair - who will then go through to the semi-final eliminator round.(n)

programme.title: Big Dreams Small Spaces
programme.desc: Monty Don helps young couple Jack and Chris in Clapham to turn their tiny urban back space into a Victorian-inspired plant collector's paradise - with a twist. However, their timing is not perfect, as they are aiming to add a special dahlia collection, a fern wall and lots of modern gardening gadgets at the same time as refurbishing their home. Meanwhile, in Hereford, daughter Frankie is trying to renovate her cottage garden in memory of her sister, with the help of her mother Pat, but her ambitious plans clash with Monty's more practical approach.(n)

programme.title: Vivacious Lady (1938)
programme.desc: A professor falls in love with a brash nightclub singer while on a trip to New York, and impulsively marries her. However, he is afraid his conservative family will not approve of his new wife, so tries to keep the relationship a secret from them. Romantic comedy, with Ginger Rogers and James Stewart.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: A collection of previously unseen finds from the show's travels round the country, visiting St Albans Cathedral, Wolverhampton Art Gallery, and Packwood House in Warwickshire.(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman.(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games

programme.title: Dig WW2 with Dan Snow
programme.desc: The historian travels to Lough Foyle to explore the history of a Flying Fortress that crashed while travelling from America to an air force base near Bedford. He unearths the stories of the crew's survival against the odds and learns how a local woman and her boat contributed to their rescue. He also visits the disused Cluntoe airfield in Co Tyrone, where RAF pilots were trained for flying missions all over Europe, and looks back at the climatic end to the Battle of the Atlantic on the River Foyle docks.(n)

programme.title: RHS Chelsea Flower Show

programme.title: Vienna Blood
programme.desc: Oskar is called away from Max and Clara's engagement party to investigate the brutal murders of three women in a brothel. Clara understands Max's eagerness to join the investigation and allows him to follow, much to the detective's dismay. The police almost immediately arrest Victor Krull, a man who regularly visited the brothel and took a particular interest in one of the victims, whose body has been washed and ritualistically displayed. The press are informed of the arrest, but Max is not at all convinced.(n)

programme.title: Comedy Shorts
programme.desc: A filmic offshoot of Tim Key's stage show Megadate, in which a man dazzles a woman on the perfect first date. However, she doesn't text him back, leading him to retrace his steps. His path is riddled with cynics and naysayers, questioning his optimistic outlook on how the date went. The nerd in the bowling alley who thought he'd embarrassed himself, the old eel catcher on the mud flats trying to douse his dreams with wisdom, and the compassionate waitress at the chicken shop. Starring Tim Key, Mandeep Dhillon, Sam Fletcher, Bill Paterson and Julie Dray.(n)

programme.title: Alastair Campbell: Depression and Me
programme.desc: The journalist and former Downing Street press secretary - best known as Tony Blair's 'spin doctor' during his tenure as prime minister - talks about his lifelong battle with depression. Encouraged by his family, Alastair sets out on a journey to explore if cutting-edge science can offer him, and the millions of people like him, the hope of one day living free of the illness. As he tries to better understand his depression, he also reflects on key events in his life and asks if they could have had a negative effect on his mind.(n)

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: The five teams are reunited and look back on their journey, recalling the highs and lows, and sharing memories of the people who helped them along the way. They also reveal what their first impressions of each other were, and how those views were changed by the challenges they all had to face.(n)

programme.title: Saved on Camera
programme.desc: A plane crash in Quebec leaves a pilot stranded, an injured hiker drags himself three kilometres to safety in the Australian Outback and a brother saves his sibling who has lost consciousness on a free dive in the Bahamas. Narrated by Kevin Duala.(n)

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: The second of two programmes following hospital staff treating Covid-19 cases features patients who are on dialysis or who have recently had a transplant, and as such are among the most vulnerable to the virus. Royal Free London faces a near catastrophic breakdown of oxygen supply, while an infectious diseases consultant starts a trial of an anti-viral drug used in the treatment of Ebola.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: Ross Kemp and Britain's Volunteer Army
programme.desc: The actor celebrates the heroic efforts of some of the UK's 750,000 volunteers during the coronavirus crisis.(n)

programme.title: Your Chelsea Flower Show
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman and the team highlight Chelsea designer ideas for supporting wildlife in your garden, and reveal some of the best flowers to attract pollinators.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: In a special edition, Fiona Bruce marks the 75th anniversary of VE Day, looking back at objects featured on the programme that gave personal insights into the momentous events of the Second World War. There is first-hand testimony from those who were present at VE Day, including the Roadshow's own Henry Sandon, while Fiona also delves into the BBC archives to discover the challenges of reporting the announcement and the celebrations across the UK and abroad.(n)

programme.title: The Great Interior Design Challenge
programme.desc: The remaining two designers battle to be crowned champion in the grand final of the competition. Standing in between them and the title is the challenge of transforming three rooms in apartments in an 18th-century mock castle in Broadstairs. To make the challenge more difficult than ever, the rooms are larger than before and for the first time, each designer will be tackling a kitchen. As the race for the title nears its end, the pair must impress Daniel Hopwood and Kelly Hoppen, as well as former judge and interior stylist Sophie Robinson. Presented by Tom Dyckhoff.(n)

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Letterbox
programme.desc: Mel Giedroyc hosts the game show in which pairs of contestants go head to head attempting to crack a series of passwords, in a bid to win £2,500.(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize.(n)

programme.title: Big Dreams Small Spaces
programme.desc: Monty Don helps Bromley couple Mei and Gerard with their ambitious plan to turn their narrow, split-level garden into a Japanese-style paradise, complete with mountain stream, mossy boulders and traditional plants. But with tons of soil to move and their budget running out, it seems they have bitten off more than they can chew. In Stoke, artists Anna and Andrew want to transform their concrete yard into a haven of tranquillity with something beautiful to see in every corner. However, they will need to use all their creative flair - not to mention Monty's expert advice - to avoid overcrowding this very small space.(n)

programme.title: Beautiful But Dangerous (1954)
programme.desc: An heiress returns to her home town to shower gifts on the people who donated money in order for her to undergo a life-saving operation when she was a child. However, the woman is not prepared for the consequences of her good deed. Comedy drama, starring Jean Simmons, Robert Mitchum, Arthur Hunnicutt and Edgar Buchanan.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antiques experts Charles Hanson and Adam Partridge unearth all manner of items to auction in Morecambe, Lancashire, including French porcelain and Japanese carvings. Plus, presenter Paul Martin visits the railway station that acted as the set for the iconic film Brief Encounter.(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman.(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Actress Chizzy Akudolu, writer and actor Charlie Higson, historian Kate Williams and comedian Tom Allen take part in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games.(n)

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: A new troop of colobus monkeys move onto Gorilla Island and keeper Rachel is locked in a battle of wills with Dave the Canadian timberwolf.(n)

programme.title: Front Row Late
programme.desc: Mary and her guests, including artistic director of the Young Vic theatre Kwame Kwei-Armah, discuss the long-term effects of Covid-19 on the future of performance, and what culture might mean once the current lockdown is lifted. There is also a performance from actors Emma Thompson and Greg Wise.(n)

programme.title: War Walks
programme.desc: Richard Holmes recalls the night of December 29, 1940, when St Paul's Cathedral and much of London suffered heavy bombing, tracing events surrounding the terrible destruction caused during the Blitz.(n)

programme.title: RHS Chelsea Flower Show
programme.sub-title: The Best of RHS Chelsea Flower Show
programme.desc: Monty Don and Joe Swift celebrate the positive power of plants as they focus on the designers and growers striving for a better world with the creations they bring to the event. They reveal how, over the years, the show has contributed to the better promotion of the health of the planet, plants and people, and talk to Countryfile presenter Ellie Harrison and BBC correspondent Fergal Keane about the importance of gardens to mental wellbeing.(n)

programme.title: What's the Matter with Tony Slattery?
programme.desc: Tony Slattery was one of the most gifted TV comedians of the late 1980s and early 1990s, only to disappear from screens in 1996 amid rumours of a massive breakdown. As he approaches 60, he has been touring the country with a show that explores his past and his mental health. Although depression was diagnosed, he and his partner Mark Hutchinson are convinced he may have bipolar disorder, so Tony wants to have his mental health reassessed. But it will mean opening up about his addictions and a painful event in his childhood.(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.sub-title: Noel Fielding, Dane Baptiste, Al Porter
programme.desc: One half of The Mighty Boosh and co-host of The Great British Bake Off Noel Fielding takes over the hosting reins of the long-running show as he comperes an evening of stand-up at London's Hammersmith Apollo. The comedian, actor, artist, DJ and musician treats the audience to his own peculiar brand of surreal humour, before introducing routines by two up-and-coming stars of the comedy circuit, with south Londoner Dane Baptiste and Irishman Al Porter taking to the stage at the famous venue.(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's national and international events, with Kirsty Wark.(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Monkman & Seagull's Genius Adventures
programme.desc: Eric Monkman and Bobby Seagull travel around Britain as they explore their favourite scientific breakthroughs, beginning with the 18th-century inventions that kick-started the Industrial Revolution and transformed scientific understanding. They head to Derbyshire to investigate Richard Arkwright's water frame - a spinning machine that allowed cotton textiles to be mass-produced, and then examineJames Watt's separate condenser, an unassuming invention that would be the biggest single improvement ever made to the steam engine.(n)

programme.title: Growing Up Gifted
programme.desc: Part two of two. The conclusion of the documentary follows three gifted teenage girls, Anne Marie, Jada and Shakira, as they make decisions about their futures. In Port Talbot, Anne Marie is more settled after moving in with her best friend, but isolation from her family and nerves about her exams are starting to trouble her. Meanwhile, would-be paediatrician Jada has her heart set on taking her A Levels at grammar school, and Tamworth-based Shakira has got to make up lost ground if she is to achieve the high grades her teachers have predicted.(n)

programme.title: Lee Miller - A Life on the Front Line
programme.desc: Lee Miller is one of the most remarkable female icons of the 20th century. A fashion model-turned-photographer, then later a war reporter, Miller chose to live life by her own rules. This documentary drama celebrates a subject who defied anyone who tried to pin her down, put her on a pedestal or pigeonhole her in any way. Often at odds with the morality of the day, she refused to be subjugated by the dominant male figures around her.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: Ross Kemp and Britain's Volunteer Army
programme.desc: The actor and film-maker celebrates the efforts of some of the UK's 750,000 volunteers, joining them as they help with everything from shopping to delivering hospital equipment.(n)

programme.title: Your Chelsea Flower Show
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman presents a selection of ideas for making the most of a budget, looking back at inexpensive landscaping techniques from past medal-winning gardens. Plus, packing borders or containers without breaking the bank and making displays from cheap bunches of flowers.(n)

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits a factory in Essex that produces 400,000 cereal bars a day, and follows the production process from the arrival of the ingredients to the eventual dispatch, discovering the importance of getting the perfect balance between honey and glucose. Cherry Healey travels to South Africa to help with the macadamia harvest, where a quarter of the world's supply of these nuts are farmed. Historian Ruth Goodman climbs a mountain in the Lake District to investigate the history of one of the oldest forms of snack bar - the Kendal Mint Cake.(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.sub-title: Puerto Rico: Island of Enchantment - Natural World
programme.desc: After being colonised by humans, Puerto Rico's rich natural resources were rapidly exploited for agriculture, industry and urban development. This documentary follows a conservation team fighting to save the most endangered creatures from extinction after they have been pushed to the edge of existence by human encroachment. David Attenborough narrates.(n)

programme.title: A Tale of Five Temples: The Story of Hinduism in Britain
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the history of five Hindu temples across Britain, tracing their stories and the spread of Hinduism in the UK. Featuring the elaborate rituals and the moving stories of Hindu worshippers, the film gives an insight into the sheer variety of Hindu beliefs across Britain. Narrated by Naga Munchetty.(n)

programme.title: Astaire and Rogers Sing the Great American Songbook
programme.desc: A compilation of hits performed by Fred Astaire and Ginger Rogers, all written by renowned American songwriters including George and Ira Gershwin, Irving Berlin, Jerome Kern and Dorothy Fields, Harold Arlen and Johnny Mercer.(n)

programme.title: Carefree (1938)
programme.desc: A woman has doubts about her impending wedding and pays a visit to her fiance's therapist friend. However, the counselling does not go to plan and she develops romantic feelings for the doctor, forcing him to resort to hypnotism to cure her infatuation - with unforeseen consequences. Musical comedy, starring Fred Astaire, Ginger Rogers and Ralph Bellamy.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin is joined by antiques experts James Lewis and Caroline Hawley at Wolverhampton Art Gallery, which boasts an extensive collection of paintings. Turning up out of the hundreds of bags and boxes is a sword with a dark history and an early Moorcroft vase. Host Paul also explores some of the region's industrial past.(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman.(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Actress Chizzy Akudolu, author and comedian Charlie Higson, historian Kate Williams and comedian Tom Allen take part in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games, culminating in the week's winner being crowned.(n)

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Longleat keeper Caleb wants to find out who are the heroes and who are the zeros in lion country with a test of their intelligence. Calling on departments from across the estate, he tries to create a contraption that will see the lions work together as a team to release their food. Meanehile, Ben Fogle tries to mend his ways with Rio the tearaway cockatoo and Longleat's very own spiderman tries to save a rare arachnid.(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Road to Mexico
programme.desc: The chef's culinary travels take him through Mexico City, and Puebla, where he samples Mexico's most iconic dish, the meat stew mole. Later, in the deserts of Oaxaca, he discovers how one of the country's most famous drinks, mezcal is made.(n)

programme.title: RHS Chelsea Flower Show
programme.sub-title: The Best of RHS Chelsea Flower Show
programme.desc: Monty Don, Joe Swift and the team look at the legends and legacies that have made the Chelsea Flower Show the greatest event in the horticultural calendar. They discover what it takes to become an icon at this show, talk to the Chelsea designers and plants men and women that have broken records, won global recognition and inspired generations of gardeners. Plus, how movers and shakers from other creative fields have been influenced by their visits to the event over the years.(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: With lockdown affecting new filming, Monty Don is at Longmeadow to take an indulgent, horticultural romp through the Gardeners' World archives. From delphiniums to clematis, irises, orchids and dahlias expert growers share their passion along with hints and tips. Plus, Carol Klein visits a garden in Shropshire where she discovers picture perfect plant combinations, Adam Frost travels to the Cotswolds and finds out what happens when plants are brought together with imagination and daring and Nick Bailey offers a guide to the world of Britain's favourite flower - the rose.(n)

programme.title: Later - with Jools Holland
programme.desc: The host reinvents his music show to accommodate the requirements of the lockdown, joined - virtually - in this edition by grime pioneer Dizzee Rascal. The east London rapper talks through his musical journey and some of the artists that were part of it, illustrated by clips from the BBC archives. His choices reflect the diverse array of genres from indie to grime and jazz, that have influenced his music, alongside memories from a career that kicked off with his Mercury Prize-winning debut Boy in Da Corner in 2003. Plus, as part of the show's focus on new music and artists, a unique performance by a contemporary artist.(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: The day's important national and international news stories, with Kirsty Wark.(n)

programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Spotlight (2015)
programme.desc: A team of investigative reporters for a Boston newspaper investigate reports of children being molested within the Catholic church. The journalists discover the situation is far worse than it seemed, as they find a conspiracy among the city's legal, religious and government establishments to cover up decades of abuse. Oscar-winning fact-based drama, starring Mark Ruffalo, Michael Keaton, Rachel McAdams and Stanley Tucci.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross paints a beautiful monochromatic scene with delicate leafless trees and a rich grassy meadow cuddling a sweet cottage.(n)

programme.title: Secrets of Skin
programme.desc: Ben Garrod explores how skin acts as a barrier between animals and the outside world, shielding them from extremes of temperature, the harmful effects of UV and a host of organisms.(n)

programme.title: Michael Wood's Story of England
programme.sub-title: Henry VIII to the Industrial Revolution
programme.desc: The broadcaster tracks Kibworth's 17th-century dissenters, explores the Grand Union Canal, a waterway that connects Birmingham with London, and discovers the achievements of a pioneering 18th-century feminist writer. The descendants of a highwayman transported to Australia uncover their roots, and the effects of the Industrial Revolution on the village are examined.(n)

programme.title: Climbing Blind
programme.desc: Documentary about Jesse Dufton and his attempt to take on the challenge of becoming the first blind person to lead a climb of the Old Man of Hoy in Orkney, Scotland. At four years of age, Jesse was diagnosed with retinitis pigmentosa, a rare genetic disease that breaks down the retina's cells. When he was 20, Jesse could no longer read. By the time he was 30, he could only detect light, with around a one to two per cent field of view. As a lifelong climber, what Jesse has achieved flies in the face of adversity, training for world cup events and leading traditional rock climbs with his sight guide and fiancee Molly. Now, his attempt on the Old Man of Hoy is testimony to his ambition to take on new and greater challenges, despite his devastating condition.(n)

programme.title: Invasion! With Sam Willis
programme.desc: Sam Willis tells the story of the Barbary Corsaire pirates who made their headquarters on Lundy Island. He also sheds light on the little-known tale of King Louis the Lion, a French King who invaded Britain in the 13th century.(n)

programme.title: The Quest for Bannockburn
programme.desc: Part two of two. Neil Oliver and Tony Pollard conclude their attempt to mark the 700th anniversary of Bannockburn by pinpointing the location of Scotland's most iconic battlefield. They are joined by historians, archaeologists and environmental scientists as they gather evidence to chart the events that ended in Robert the Bruce's overwhelming victory over the English that helped seal Scotland's future as an independent kingdom.(n)

programme.title: A Timewatch Guide
programme.sub-title: The Mary Rose: A Timewatch Guide
programme.desc: Historian Dan Snow explores the discovery, excavation and raising of the ship, finding out what the latest research has revealed about the vessel and her crew. The presenter also investigates how the project helped create modern underwater archaeology, and examines the techniques, challenges and successes of the divers and archaeologists involved.(n)

programme.title: The Art of Japanese Life
programme.desc: James Fox explores the art of the Japanese home, the clean minimalism of which has been exported round the world from modernist architecture to lifestyle stores.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross paints a beautiful monochromatic scene with delicate leafless trees and a rich grassy meadow cuddling a sweet cottage.(n)

programme.title: David Stratton's Stories of Australian Cinema
programme.desc: How variations on the theme of family are depicted in Australian movie-making The Castle's nuclear, if unorthodox family, a family of faith in The Devil's Playground, Romper Stomper's frightening Neo-Nazis and crime families such as those depicted in Ned Kelly and Animal Kingdom.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers further advice as he paints another picture. Today he creates a mountain scene with soft, sloping grass cover, crystal lake and rocky banks.(n)

programme.title: Secrets of Skin
programme.desc: Biologist Ben Garrod examines how animals use their skin to send out messages to their own species, and warning signals to others.(n)

programme.title: Pride and Prejudice
programme.desc: Elizabeth visits Mr Collins once Charlotte has accepted his proposal of marriage and confides in Jane that she regards him as one of the stupidest men in England. Mrs Bennet sizes up George Wickham as a potential suitor for her daughter - but he has set his heart on society heiress Miss King.(n)

programme.title: Earth from Space
programme.desc: Cameras in space reveal the stories behind Earth's most remarkable patterns and shapes, including fresh water lagoons in Brazil, mysterious light spots in the Australian outback, endless green canopy in the Congo rainforest, and the twists and turns of the Amazon river which provides a home for rehabilitated manatees.(n)

programme.title: Chasing the Moon
programme.sub-title: A Place Beyond the Sky - Part Two
programme.desc: Under Wernher von Braun's leadership, America's technology finally seemed to be catching up with the Soviet Union's. On 5 May 1961, his Redstone rocket successfully launched American navy test pilot Alan Shepard 116 vertical miles into space. The American space programme grew rapidly.(n)

programme.title: Science and Islam
programme.desc: Professor Jim Al-Khalili reveals how Polish astronomer Nicolaus Copernicus's theories about the Earth's orbit were shaped by the discoveries of Islamic scientists. He travels to Syria to observe Ibn Al-Shatir's sundial on the minaret of the Umayyad Mosque in Damascus, before visiting one of the most influential observatories of the Islamic world, Maragheh designed by Al-Tusi. He ends his journey at the Royan Institute in Iran, to investigate how science is now regarded in Islam.(n)

programme.title: The Art of Gothic: Britain's Midnight Hour
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon examines England's Gothic Revival - an architectural movement that began in the mid-1700s. He starts by exploring its restrained aristocratic beginnings, then reveals how it went on to influence some of Britain's most famous artwork and buildings.(n)

programme.title: Sleuths, Spies & Sorcerers: Andrew Marr's Paperback Heroes
programme.desc: The broadcaster explores the conventions of the fantasy genre, profiling key figures including JRR Tolkien, CS Lewis and Terry Pratchett and chatting to best-selling writers Neil Gaiman, Alan Garner and Frances Hardinge.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers further advice as he paints another picture. Today he creates a mountain scene with soft, sloping grass cover, crystal lake and rocky banks.(n)

programme.title: Earth from Space
programme.desc: Cameras in space reveal the stories behind Earth's most remarkable patterns and shapes, including fresh water lagoons in Brazil, mysterious light spots in the Australian outback, endless green canopy in the Congo rainforest, and the twists and turns of the Amazon river which provides a home for rehabilitated manatees.(n)

programme.title: Dazzling Duets at the BBC
programme.desc: Memorable performances from the BBC archive, featuring singers and musicians from various genres on stage together in shows ranging from Parkinson to the Proms. Featuring appearances by Oscar Peterson, Kiri te Kanawa, Nigel Kennedy, Larry Adler, Bela Fleck and Ballake Sissoko.(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989

programme.desc: With cinemas closed and the nation on lockdown, Mark Kermode and Simon Mayo turn their attention to the small screen, presenting a guide to the best - and worst - of streaming culture, across movies and premium TV. Here, they take a critical look at The First Team and I Know This Much Is True.(n)

programme.title: A Musical History
programme.desc: A multi-faceted genius whose music has permeated popular culture, Stevie Wonder is not short of celebrity fans. Contributors include actor Martin Freeman, singers Alexander O'Neal, James Morrison, Beverley Knight and Corinne Bailey Rae, New Order's Gillian Gilbert and Stephen Morris, DJs Ana Matronic, Trevor Nelson and Norman Jay, Heaven's 17's Glenn Gregory and Martyn Ware, journalist Sian Pattenden and presenter Emma Dabiri.(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the restorers get to work on more family heirlooms, including a battered, weather-beaten dictionary believed to have been the personal possession of Lord Horatio Nelson and used on board his flagship, HMS Victory. programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The amateur sewer competition hosted by Joe Lycett continues. This time, the eight remaining contestants take part in a series of challenges as they return to the sewing room for lingerie and sleepwear week. To test the sewers' handling skills, judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young kick off the pattern challenge with a boned basque. The task requires sewing together small pattern pieces for a precise fit and creating a tricky hook and eye fastening. Next, in the transformation challenge, the sewers transform pyjamas and nightwear into a summer dress, before finally being tasked to create a two-piece female sleep set which has to incorporates lace somewhere in their design.(n)

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.desc: Documentary following two men preparing to compete in the World Stone Skimming Championships, held on the tiny west coast island of Easdale in Scotland. Dougie is eight-time World Stone Skimming champion and native Scot from Blairgowrie, and although he is confident, newcomer Alex threatens his legendary status. The programme charts the determination and dedication of the competitors, and reveals what it truly takes to come out on top in this unique contest.(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Great Britain sprinter Adam Gemili, former Premier League midfielder Steve Sidwell, Commonwealth gold medal-winning swimmer Aimee Willmott and world champion gymnast Joe Fraser join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz.(n)

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The amateur sewer competition hosted by Joe Lycett continues. This time, the eight remaining contestants take part in a series of challenges as they return to the sewing room for lingerie and sleepwear week. To test the sewers' handling skills, judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young kick off the pattern challenge with a boned basque. The task requires sewing together small pattern pieces for a precise fit and creating a tricky hook and eye fastening. Next, in the transformation challenge, the sewers transform pyjamas and nightwear into a summer dress, before finally being tasked to create a two-piece female sleep set which has to incorporates lace somewhere in their design.(n)

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: Documentary-maker Stacey Dooley is stuck in her flat like many of the UK. But armed with social media and a network of contacts, she reaches out to people across the country in search of those who are helping others. They include Wigan teacher-turned-scrub maker Charlotte, who works 14-hour days to provide protective equipment for health services; charity worker Freya, who chose to continue her work in Greece rather than fly home to be with her mother; and Glasgow man Jonathan, just one of the volunteers providing food for the city's homeless.(n)

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles meets 47-year-old Stuart in Torquay, who has progressive multiple sclerosis. Despite being six foot seven, he sleeps in a chair in the living room. His primary carer is his 73-year-old mother, Grandma Lin, who is completely blind, and walks across town every day to see to her son and her granddaughter Lauren's needs. Can the crack team of builders, plasterers and electricians help turn their lives around?(n)

programme.desc: There are potentially double the listeners for Kurupt FM, while duty calls for Beats when Roche goes into labour. Steves has plans with his Nan, leaving Grindah to run the station, and Miche wonders whether there is any chance of her getting back together with her ex.(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Great Britain sprinter Adam Gemili, former Premier League midfielder Steve Sidwell, Commonwealth gold medal-winning swimmer Aimee Willmott and world champion gymnast Joe Fraser join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz.(n)

programme.title: Islam, Women and Me
programme.desc: Mehreen Baig finds that everyone has an opinion on how she should live her life and, in this documentary asks if it is possible to be a strong, independent woman and a good Muslim. She meets the young women who claim Islam is a feminist religion that empowers them, and a woman who has rejected the religion because she believes to be inherently sexist.(n)

programme.desc: The efforts of volunteers during the coronavirus crisis. Ross joins volunteers at the Rotary Club in Maidenhead, who are delivering food parcels to local hungry households.(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The development progress of a flat in Folkestone, Kent and a house in Earby, Lancashire, as well as a revisit to a West Midlands commercial premises first featured in 2016.(n)

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A drone is used in the search for a British climber missing in the Himalayas. A mother panics when she sees a news picture of her son's wrecked car and cannot get hold of him.(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Kate Bliss help two teams look for items in Stamford Meadows, Lincolnshire, while Natasha Raskin Sharp has advice on how to identify different kinds of wood.(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Eve helps Al scores a victory against his childhood bully. Valerie's quiet night in is thwarted by a cry for help, and Karen cringes when Rob tucks in to her ‘special' brownies.(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.sub-title: The Sign of the Broken Sword
programme.desc: A murder is committed at the local army barracks using a legendary broken sword, and the incident leads the priest to investigate another death at Dunkirk 13 years previously. Fifties-set crime drama based on the stories by GK Chesterton, starring Mark Williams.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a woman and her niece to search for a house in rural Gloucestershire, and also visits Owlpen Manor to find out how the Tudor property was rescued.(n)

programme.title: Your Chelsea Flower Show
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman presents a selection of ideas for making the most of a budget, looking back at inexpensive landscaping techniques from past medal-winning gardens. Plus, packing borders or containers without breaking the bank and making displays from cheap bunches of flowers.(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones co-hosts the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest.(n)

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: Documentary-maker Stacey Dooley is stuck in her flat like many of the UK. But armed with social media and a network of contacts, she reaches out to people across the country in search of those who are helping others. They include Wigan teacher-turned-scrub maker Charlotte, who works 14-hour days to provide protective equipment for health services; charity worker Freya, who chose to continue her work in Greece rather than fly home to be with her mother; and Glasgow man Jonathan, just one of the volunteers providing food for the city's homeless.(n)

programme.title: Coronavirus: Clap for Our Carers
programme.desc: Live coverage as people around the UK applaud health service workers battling the coronavirus pandemic.(n)

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles meets 47-year-old Stuart in Torquay, who has progressive multiple sclerosis. Despite being six foot seven, he sleeps in a chair in the living room. His primary carer is his 73-year-old mother, Grandma Lin, who is completely blind, and walks across town every day to see to her son and her granddaughter Lauren's needs. Can the crack team of builders, plasterers and electricians help turn their lives around?(n)

programme.title: The Real Marigold Hotel
programme.desc: The friends go all out to make Susie's birthday a night to remember, and as the celebration begin, Britt is keen to show she still has what it takes by showing off her singing and dancing talents. The senior citizens also head to Chennai, where Zandra is reunited with a friend from 40 years ago and Henry relives some difficult memories of his last overseas broadcast at the city's famous cricket ground. Back in Puducherry, John and Duncan test their fitness by jumping on their bikes, while Paul, revelling in the chaos of the Indian roads, decides to take his mock Indian driving test.(n)

programme.desc: The efforts of volunteers during the coronavirus crisis. Ross meets residents in Berkshire who pulled together to help those in need. The volunteering efforts of the street include pharmacy and post office runs, shopping, food bank collections and poem recitals.(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts is in Tylorstown, south Wales, looking around a double-fronted end terrace, with some damp issues. He's also in Rainham in Kent to see a Dutch barn-style house. Martel Maxwell is just outside Crewe to see a Grade II listed house.(n)

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A man fights for his life when what appeared to be a sore throat worsens and becomes sepsis. A historic home and restaurant is engulfed in flames, but a young man sleeps through the fire alarm.(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Roo Irvine and Tim Weeks help the red and blue teams in Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, where they hope to bag bargains to take to auction to sell at a profit. Presenter Charlie Ross also finds out about the fascinating world of espionage and uncovers some everyday objects that aren't quite what they seem.(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Al cannot shake his worry for Eve, while Ayesha and Bear's organ donor campaign finds a stumbling block in Zara. Sid helps an elderly woman when an overzealous estate agent takes advantage.(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The priest finds himself in a seemingly impossible situation when he is told during a confession that Bishop Talbot will be assassinated tomorrow. Fifties-set crime drama based on the stories by GK Chesterton, starring Mark Williams.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah is on a house-hunting mission in the Forest of Dean helping a couple with a £500,000 budget find a perfect country home that meets their specific demands for the garden. While in the area, Sonali visits Ragmans Lane Farm and discovers how market gardening produces not just organic food, but is also a financial leg-up for small-scale veg growers trying to break into the farming industry.(n)

programme.title: Your Chelsea Flower Show
programme.desc: The Rich Brothers, Rachel de Thame, Toby Buckland and Frances Tophill reveal some of the most beautiful flowers that are really easy to grow and those plants that need virtually no attention. Plus, ideas for low maintenance lawns, border planting that takes very little effort and why spending very little time on orchids could make them even prettier. Presented by Nicki Chapman.(n)

programme.desc: A selection of the best moments from the series, as team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker are joined by a selection of sporting stars.(n)

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Susan celebrates her 40th birthday with Ben in a small guesthouse they stayed at 20 years ago. However, a pair of ageing swingers ensure not everything goes to plan. Back at home, the children are left under Nick's supervision, which consists of helping Janey bribe Michael to keep quiet about the weekend's activities.(n)

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee thinks the twins are too young to be taught about sex at school and that their teacher Miss Anstis is too stuck up to teach it. He resolves that it is his duty as a father to teach his children everything he knows about the facts of life, discovering only when it is too late that his knowledge is somewhat limited. Comedy, starring Lee Mack and Sally Bretton.(n)

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Charlie Brooker hosts the satirical current affairs quiz, with regular team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton joined by panellists Dr Hannah Fry and Mark Steel.(n)

programme.title: Peter Kay's Car Share
programme.desc: An unfortunate event at work means John and Kayleigh get to take the morning off together, allowing them to get to know each other a little bit better. However, the assistant manager is shocked when his passenger reveals what she gets up to on an evening with her elderly neighbour. Sitcom, starring Peter Kay and Sian Gibson.(n)

programme.title: Tropic Thunder (2008)
programme.desc: A director's epic Vietnam movie is behind schedule and over budget. Taking the advice of the grizzled war veteran on whose life the film is based, he drops the actors into a jungle with the intention of filming them fighting guerrilla-style - but the cast is unaware the surrounding warfare is genuine. Comedy, directed by and starring Ben Stiller, with Jack Black, Steve Coogan and, in an Oscar-nominated role, Robert Downey Jr.(n)

programme.title: Absolutely India: Mancs in Mumbai
programme.desc: Manchester-born brothers Ryan, Adam and Scott Thomas embark on an epic journey to trace their family roots in India, joined by their dad Dougie, whose father was born in Mumbai. However, the trio have never visited the country they descended from. In the fourth episode, the three brothers track down another branch of their growing family tree, before being put through their paces in a brutal yoga session. Later, they travel to a place renowned for its spiritual enlightenment, but a therapeutic conversation takes a decidedly different turn as the brothers finally get to have their say and talk to Dougie about how they felt when he left the family home when they were children.(n)

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: The comedian follows the work of Battersea Dogs & Cats Home in London, meeting staff involved in treating, training and rehoming animals. This time, Paul meets a 10-month-old puppy who has been brought into Battersea twice for being too boisterous, before helping to soothe a pointer cross who has had a hard life.(n)

programme.title: Harry's Heroes: Euro Having a Laugh
programme.desc: In the final episode, Harry, John and the team embark on the final leg of the tour in Germany, as they prepare to face their arch-rivals at the Wacker Arena in Burghausen. After checking into their Hotel in the Bavarian Alps, Harry receives the German team sheet, leaving him concerned his team are going to have to massively step up their game. With the fear of the match going to penalties hanging over their head, Harry brings in a German psychologist to offer some advice. Finally the big day arrives, and Harry has managed to recruit Manchester United legend Teddy Sheringham, so the team finally has its missing striker.(n)

programme.title: Tonight
programme.sub-title: The Race for a Cure: Making Britain Safe - Tonight
programme.desc: Ranj Singh looks at the urgent race to find drugs to successfully treat Covid-19. With hopes of defeating the deadly virus lying with the world's scientists - including those working in Britain - Dr Singh gains first-hand access to laboratories and drug trials across the country. He reports on the unprecedented scientific efforts going into developing drugs to treat those in hospital with the virus, as well as the search for a vaccine to inoculate everyone. With the success of these trials determining how soon people can resume normal life and get the economy running as before, the stakes could not be higher.(n)

programme.title: The Martin Lewis Money Show
programme.sub-title: The Martin Lewis Money Show Live
programme.desc: The financial journalist presents up-to-the-minute advice for people who have found themselves living with financial uncertainty. Live from his house, Martin answers viewers' questions on how the economic crisis affects family finances, jobs and consumer rights.(n)

programme.title: Secrets of the Driving Test
programme.desc: Cocksure 17-year-old Bethany is barely out of the test centre before getting into a game of chicken. Drew and Julie are back and eager to improve on their catastrophic first tests, while in Blackpool, retired policeman Philip is hoping a pass will finally stop his wife telling him how to drive.(n)

programme.title: Inside the Crown: Secrets of the Royals
programme.desc: Documentary revealing how Queen Elizabeth II has mastered the art of being a monarch, both home and abroad, over her record-breaking 68-year reign. She is seen meeting world leaders such as Ronald Reagan, Mikhail Gorbachev and Donald Trump - although not all encounters go well, and the film also looks at the Queen's commitment to the Commonwealth, a personal project that has seen the old Empire transformed.(n)

programme.title: Absolutely India: Mancs in Mumbai
programme.desc: Manchester-born brothers Ryan, Adam and Scott Thomas embark on an epic journey to trace their family roots in India, joined by their dad Dougie, whose father was born in Mumbai. However, the trio have never visited the country they descended from. In the fourth episode, the three brothers track down another branch of their growing family tree, before being put through their paces in a brutal yoga session. Later, they travel to a place renowned for its spiritual enlightenment, but a therapeutic conversation takes a decidedly different turn as the brothers finally get to have their say and talk to Dougie about how they felt when he left the family home when they were children.(n)

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: The comedian follows the work of Battersea Dogs & Cats Home in London, meeting staff involved in treating, training and rehoming animals. programme.title: Harry's Heroes: Euro Having a Laugh
programme.desc: In the final episode, Harry, John and the team embark on the final leg of the tour in Germany, as they prepare to face their arch-rivals at the Wacker Arena in Burghausen. After checking into their Hotel in the Bavarian Alps, Harry receives the German team sheet, leaving him concerned his team are going to have to massively step up their game. With the fear of the match going to penalties hanging over their head, Harry brings in a German psychologist to offer some advice. Finally the big day arrives, and Harry has managed to recruit Manchester United legend Teddy Sheringham, so the team finally has its missing striker.(n)

programme.title: Tonight
programme.sub-title: The Race for a Cure: Making Britain Safe - Tonight
programme.desc: Ranj Singh looks at the urgent race to find drugs to successfully treat Covid-19. With hopes of defeating the deadly virus lying with the world's scientists - including those working in Britain - Dr Singh gains first-hand access to laboratories and drug trials across the country. He reports on the unprecedented scientific efforts going into developing drugs to treat those in hospital with the virus, as well as the search for a vaccine to inoculate everyone. With the success of these trials determining how soon people can resume normal life and get the economy running as before, the stakes could not be higher.(n)

programme.title: Inside the Crown: Secrets of the Royals
programme.desc: Documentary revealing how Queen Elizabeth II has mastered the art of being a monarch, both home and abroad, over her record-breaking 68-year reign. She is seen meeting world leaders such as Ronald Reagan, Mikhail Gorbachev and Donald Trump - although not all encounters go well, and the film also looks at the Queen's commitment to the Commonwealth, a personal project that has seen the old Empire transformed.(n)

programme.desc: A disgruntled client falls under suspicion when an insurance salesman is found murdered, but the victim's complicated private life gives Mason a lot to think about before the case is solved. Alf Ventress makes himself at home in the Aidensfield Arms while his house undergoes repairs, and soon overstays his welcome, while a drunk locked up in the cells for a night turns out to be a war hero friend of Sgt Miller fallen on hard times.(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: The season of goodwill descends on the Larkin household, and the family celebrates the 'perfick' Christmas. Drama, based on the characters created by HE Bates, starring David Jason, Pam Ferris and Catherine Zeta-Jones.(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin and Jo become concerned when Chrissy starts dating an older man - so they decide to play gooseberry. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective's friend Sir Charles Cartwright (guest star Martin Shaw) hosts a cocktail party at his home in Cornwall, where an amiable local priest chokes to death on his drink. There seems to be no motive for murder, and no trace of poison found in the victim's glass, but weeks later, when Sir Charles's doctor friend Sir Bartholomew Strange dies in similar circumstances, he and Poirot team up to investigate. Drama, with Art Malik, Jane Asher and Anna Carteret, and David Suchet in the title role.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Sergeant Nokes arrives to take charge after a lorry driver is savagely beaten and his consignment of condemned meat is stolen on the way to the incinerator, causing a health alert in Aidensfield. Peggy gets involved when she is asked to house-sit Mrs Grant's mansion and takes advantage of the opportunity to run a makshift B&B, only to find herself under arrest for handling stolen goods.(n)

programme.desc: Sarah panics when Chris takes Joseph. Kim insists that Dave moves in with her at Home Farm. The fight night goes ahead, but not as planned.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot

programme.title: Heartbeat

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Cora's attempts at matchmaking are dashed when Mary is smitten not by intended target Evelyn Napier, but by his handsome friend, Turkish attache Kemal Pamuk. The couple's mutual attraction sparks a train of events whose consequences ripple through the entire household. Gwen is humiliated as her secret ambition is exposed, and Bates takes extreme action to cure his limp. Costume drama, starring Maggie Smith, Michelle Dockery, Brendan Patricks, Theo James, Rose Leslie and Elizabeth McGovern.(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: When a housewife is found hanged in her home, the police initially mark it down as suicide. However, a meeting with an Oxford law professor prompts a sceptical Lewis and Hathaway to believe something more sinister is afoot - a suspicion confirmed by the death of another woman. Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox star.(n)

programme.title: Bancroft
programme.desc: Free from the restrictions of her position, Elizabeth is determined to bring the true murderer to some form of justice. Armed with information to solve the case, she heads straight into the heart of the viper's nest. In a disturbing confrontation, she is forced to face her own demons. Crime thriller, starring Sarah Parish, David Avery and Charlotte Hope.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Mischievous artist Harry Field, who specialises in preparing bogus coats of arms for gullible American tourists, is murdered - and Morse takes a personal interest in finding the culprit. But as investigations progress, he discovers the victim was not quite the lovable rogue he initially appeared. John Thaw stars, with Kevin Whately, Geraldine James and Freddie Jones.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth investigates the murder of her old friend Father Gorman, using a list of names sent by the priest moments before his death to bring the guilty party to justice. Following a trail of clues to London, Marple is drawn to the Pale Horse Inn - where she discovers a sinister world of occult practices. Drama, starring Julia McKenzie in the title role, with Lynda Baron, Pauline Collins, Nicholas Parsons and Neil Pearson.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot

David Suchet, Zoe Wanamaker and Greta Scacchi star.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Mason thwarts a gang of safe-robbers and finds a bag of explosives in their abandoned van - but when the haul ends up in the wrong hands, it could spell disaster for Dawn's birthday party. Meanwhile, there are embarrassing consequences for Oscar when he investigates Wetherby's mysterious lunchtime disappearances. Guest starring James Gaddas, with Joe McFadden, Derek Fowlds and Rupert Ward-Lewis.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim and Frank row about him cancelling her credit card. Ned insists Dave stays away from them as his actions could see them lose the farm. Seth is attacked by two poachers.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Seth lies injured where the poachers left him. Mandy is determined to apply for Frank's housekeeper's job. Emma has a shock in store for Zoe.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jim has his vasectomy. Norris enjoys making Derek feel uncomfortable during his review meeting. Josie packs her bags and tells Don she's moving out.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The Duckworths throw a birthday party for Tommy. Don offers an olive branch to Nick. Des phones Steph up and invites her round.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth falls victim to depression after failing to prevent the murder of a society girl by notorious art thief Marrascaud. His confidence shattered, Poirot eventually returns to work when a lonely chauffeur begs him to find his missing soulmate, the maid of a famous Russian dancer. The investigation takes him to the Swiss Alps, where, against all odds, he stumbles upon a hotel thought to be Marrascaud's hideout - leading to a very personal showdown. The penultimate episode of the long-running detective drama, starring David Suchet, with Simon Callow, Orla Brady, Patrick Ryecart, Nigel Lindsay, Morven Christie, Tom Austen and Rupert Evans.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Mason thwarts a gang of safe-robbers and finds a bag of explosives in their abandoned van - but when the haul ends up in the wrong hands, it could spell disaster for Dawn's birthday party. Meanwhile, there are embarrassing consequences for Oscar when he investigates Wetherby's mysterious lunchtime disappearances. Guest starring James Gaddas, with Joe McFadden, Derek Fowlds and Rupert Ward-Lewis.(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: The fair arrives in the village and Mrs Hughes finds herself the centre of speculation when she meets a former suitor who makes her question her position at Downton. Meanwhile, Violet and Isobel lock horns over a medical ailment affecting Molesley, and Carson fears there is a thief at work in the grounds. Costume drama, starring Maggie Smith, Hugh Bonneville, Phyllis Logan, Penelope Wilton, Jim Carter and Kevin Doyle.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Bullard asks Barnaby to investigate the disappearance of a friend's daughter who was a member of the Oblong Foundation, a cult based at Malham Hall. Jones goes under cover as a new recruit to solve the mystery, but he and Barnaby soon start digging into an old case involving the death of a couple in a boat explosion, which could be linked to the missing woman. Detective drama, starring Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes, with Will Knightley, Kevin Doyle, Holly Aird, Barbara Flynn and John Woodvine.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Former nanny Lavinia Seagram is found soaked in blood and it is believed she had turned to drink and drugs to commit suicide. However, Poirot is soon on the scent of troubled heiress Norma Restarick, who had problems of her own - not least confessing to the possibility she has killed someone herself. Guest starring Jemima Rooper, Zoe Wanamaker and Peter Bowles, with David Suchet as the Belgian sleuth.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford sleuth investigates a double mystery connected to the local Greek community - the death of a restaurant chef and the disappearance of a baby - and wonders whether an academic event attended by some of the university's leading classical scholars could shed light on the case. Mystery, starring John Thaw and Kevin Whately, with Jan Harvey, Martin Jarvis and James Hazeldine.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.sub-title: Peril at End House - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of two. While holidaying in Cornwall with his friend Captain Hastings, the sleuth meets a local woman who has seemingly become unaccountably accident-prone - but soon starts to suspect she is actually a victim of foul play. David Suchet and Hugh Fraser star.(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.sub-title: Two Foot Two, Eyes of Blue
programme.desc: Robin and Chrissy go babysitting, but the baby will not stop crying, and the drinks cupboard is locked. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan and Paula Wilcox.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth falls victim to depression after failing to prevent the murder of a society girl by notorious art thief Marrascaud. His confidence shattered, Poirot eventually returns to work when a lonely chauffeur begs him to find his missing soulmate, the maid of a famous Russian dancer. programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The arrival of a traumatised and wounded stranger in Aidensfield coincides with the discovery of a woman's body on the moors, and sets off a treacherous chain of events that leaves one long-time resident's life hanging in the balance. Nicholas Farrell and Clare Holman guest star in the last-ever episode of the long-running police drama.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Tina is sceptical about Mandy's chances of getting the housekeeper's job. Zak is confident he can beat Arthur 'The Destroyer' Simpkins. An angry Zoe confronts Kim.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Ned is proud of Jan for attacking Kim. Zak frightens Eric into giving him a share of the profits from the fight. Zoe tells Frank about the church blessing.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Phyllis warns Des against getting involved with Steph again. Betty finds her house ransacked by burglars. Leo asks Raquel out for dinner.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maxine spends the night at Des's. Don tells Martin he intends to fight Gail to keep his home. Raquel is thrilled when Leo asks her out again. Steph turns up to see Des.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: David Suchet takes on his final case as the Belgian sleuth in the last-ever episode of the long-running drama. Crippled with arthritis, Poirot is now using a wheelchair and has a life-threatening heart condition, but remains as sharp as ever. Calling on old friend Captain Hastings for assistance, he returns to Styles, where the pair first met 30 years earlier on a murder investigation. The detective is convinced the country house is once again harbouring a killer, and as guests fall victim to a mysterious attacker, he must summon the last of his strength to battle his ultimate nemesis. With Hugh Fraser, Anne Reid, Philip Glenister, Helen Baxendale, Matthew McNulty, Anna Madeley and Shaun Dingwall.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The arrival of a traumatised and wounded stranger in Aidensfield coincides with the discovery of a woman's body on the moors, and sets off a treacherous chain of events that leaves one long-time resident's life hanging in the balance. Nicholas Farrell and Clare Holman guest star in the last-ever episode of the long-running police drama.(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: As the village prepares for the annual flower show, Isobel finds herself in conflict with Violet, and Mary is introduced to a potential suitor, but it would seem she only has eyes for Matthew. Anna realises who is to blame when a valuable snuff box goes missing, and Mrs Patmore tearfully reveals her fears to Carson that her career may be coming to an end. Meanwhile, O'Brien engineers a way for Edith to take revenge on Mary. Drama, starring Maggie Smith and Robert Bathurst.(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Aunt Joan reveals she has a rare bird nesting on her land but wants to keep it a secret to deter prospective egg thieves. Sure enough, her tactless nephew opens his mouth to the local baker, who spies an opportunity to save his ailing business by making it a new attraction. Before long the peaceful community of Portwenn is over-run by twitchers eager to catch a sight of the bird, and Dr Ellingham finds himself in the locals' bad books yet again. Martin Clunes stars in this Christmas special of the comedy drama, originally shown in 2006.(n)

programme.sub-title: Peril at End House - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of two. While holidaying in Cornwall with his friend Captain Hastings, the sleuth meets a local woman who has seemingly become unaccountably accident-prone - but soon starts to suspect she is actually a victim of foul play. David Suchet and Hugh Fraser star.(n)

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service.(n)
