programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jim has his vasectomy. Norris enjoys making Derek feel uncomfortable during his review meeting. Josie packs her bags and tells Don she's moving out.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The Duckworths throw a birthday party for Tommy. Don offers an olive branch to Nick. Des phones Steph up and invites her round.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim and Frank row about him cancelling her credit card. Ned insists Dave stays away from them as his actions could see them lose the farm. Seth is attacked by two poachers.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Seth lies injured where the poachers left him. Mandy is determined to apply for Frank's housekeeper's job. Emma has a shock in store for Zoe.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Mason thwarts a gang of safe-robbers and finds a bag of explosives in their abandoned van - but when the haul ends up in the wrong hands, it could spell disaster for Dawn's birthday party. Meanwhile, there are embarrassing consequences for Oscar when he investigates Wetherby's mysterious lunchtime disappearances. Guest starring James Gaddas, with Joe McFadden, Derek Fowlds and Rupert Ward-Lewis.(n)

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: Comedy, starring Yootha Joyce and Brian Murphy.(n)

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: Comedy, starring Yootha Joyce and Brian Murphy.(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.sub-title: Two Foot Two, Eyes of Blue
programme.desc: Robin and Chrissy go babysitting, but the baby will not stop crying, and the drinks cupboard is locked. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan and Paula Wilcox.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth falls victim to depression after failing to prevent the murder of a society girl by notorious art thief Marrascaud. His confidence shattered, Poirot eventually returns to work when a lonely chauffeur begs him to find his missing soulmate, the maid of a famous Russian dancer. The investigation takes him to the Swiss Alps, where, against all odds, he stumbles upon a hotel thought to be Marrascaud's hideout - leading to a very personal showdown. The penultimate episode of the long-running detective drama, starring David Suchet, with Simon Callow, Orla Brady, Patrick Ryecart, Nigel Lindsay, Morven Christie, Tom Austen and Rupert Evans.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The arrival of a traumatised and wounded stranger in Aidensfield coincides with the discovery of a woman's body on the moors, and sets off a treacherous chain of events that leaves one long-time resident's life hanging in the balance. Nicholas Farrell and Clare Holman guest star in the last-ever episode of the long-running police drama.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Tina is sceptical about Mandy's chances of getting the housekeeper's job. Zak is confident he can beat Arthur 'The Destroyer' Simpkins. An angry Zoe confronts Kim.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Ned is proud of Jan for attacking Kim. Zak frightens Eric into giving him a share of the profits from the fight. Zoe tells Frank about the church blessing.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Phyllis warns Des against getting involved with Steph again. Betty finds her house ransacked by burglars. Leo asks Raquel out for dinner.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maxine spends the night at Des's. Don tells Martin he intends to fight Gail to keep his home. Raquel is thrilled when Leo asks her out again. Steph turns up to see Des.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: David Suchet takes on his final case as the Belgian sleuth in the last-ever episode of the long-running drama. Crippled with arthritis, Poirot is now using a wheelchair and has a life-threatening heart condition, but remains as sharp as ever. Calling on old friend Captain Hastings for assistance, he returns to Styles, where the pair first met 30 years earlier on a murder investigation. The detective is convinced the country house is once again harbouring a killer, and as guests fall victim to a mysterious attacker, he must summon the last of his strength to battle his ultimate nemesis. With Hugh Fraser, Anne Reid, Philip Glenister, Helen Baxendale, Matthew McNulty, Anna Madeley and Shaun Dingwall.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The arrival of a traumatised and wounded stranger in Aidensfield coincides with the discovery of a woman's body on the moors, and sets off a treacherous chain of events that leaves one long-time resident's life hanging in the balance. Nicholas Farrell and Clare Holman guest star in the last-ever episode of the long-running police drama.(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: As the village prepares for the annual flower show, Isobel finds herself in conflict with Violet, and Mary is introduced to a potential suitor, but it would seem she only has eyes for Matthew. Anna realises who is to blame when a valuable snuff box goes missing, and Mrs Patmore tearfully reveals her fears to Carson that her career may be coming to an end. Meanwhile, O'Brien engineers a way for Edith to take revenge on Mary. Drama, starring Maggie Smith and Robert Bathurst.(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Aunt Joan reveals she has a rare bird nesting on her land but wants to keep it a secret to deter prospective egg thieves. Sure enough, her tactless nephew opens his mouth to the local baker, who spies an opportunity to save his ailing business by making it a new attraction. Before long the peaceful community of Portwenn is over-run by twitchers eager to catch a sight of the bird, and Dr Ellingham finds himself in the locals' bad books yet again. Martin Clunes stars in this Christmas special of the comedy drama, originally shown in 2006.(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: When a housewife is found hanged in her home, the police initially mark it down as suicide. However, a meeting with an Oxford law professor prompts a sceptical Lewis and Hathaway to believe something more sinister is afoot - a suspicion confirmed by the death of another woman. Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox star.(n)

programme.title: The Protectors
programme.desc: A reclusive industrial magnate asks Harry to engineer his brother's escape from a prison on a Mediterranean island, where he is being held on a trumped-up charge. Robert Vaughn, Nyree Dawn Porter and Tony Anholt star.(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: The coroner sets out to improve his bedside manner by spending time with a doctor, learning a valuable lesson in humility from a mother dying of a terminal illness.(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: Crocker's future is in jeopardy after he accidentally shoots a woman while trying to stop a robbery. Directed by Telly Savalas, Christopher Walken and Carol Lynley guest star.(n)

programme.title: The Return of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: The youthful heir to a fortune disappears from boarding school in the dead of night - but his aristocratic father's aversion to publicity makes the case particularly difficult to investigate. Period drama, starring Jeremy Brett.(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur is conned into buying an East German car, a retired greyhound and a piano. He fears the items could destroy his reputation, leaving him with the job of selling them on without the rest of the manor finding out. Vintage comedy drama, starring George Cole and Gary Webster.(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: When a foreign student, an apparent cocaine trafficker, is shot dead, Cowley tasks Bodie and Doyle with targetting teenage traffickers who have diplomatic ties with South America. Drama, starring Martin Shaw, Lewis Collins, Gordon Jackson and Alice Krige.(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A trusted informant tips Regan off about an upcoming robbery, but the gang involved kidnaps his daughter to blackmail him into taking no action. Police drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman.(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: The coroner is outraged when he learns that a wealthy lawyer is likely to get away with a light sentence after running over a pedestrian.(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: Kojak does not approve of a female detective joining his team, but they must work together to bring in a murderous drug dealer. Guest starring Joan Van Ark (Knots Landing).(n)

programme.title: The Return of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: The prime minister asks Holmes to investigate the disappearance of a letter which could lead to a major European catastrophe. Unfortunately, completion of the task requires him to jeopardise a titled lady's reputation - something he is naturally reluctant to do. Victorian detective drama, starring Jeremy Brett.(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur spies a business opportunity as he and Ray prepare to pay their respects to the late Charlie Johnson, but the deceased's widow has other ideas. Comedy drama, starring George Cole and Gary Webster.(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: A notorious hard-nosed villain offers to co-operate with the police and gives himself up in return for protection for his wife and their children, so Bodie and Doyle are assigned to perform the task. Crime drama, starring Lewis Collins, Martin Shaw and Gordon Jackson.(n)

programme.title: Banks of England
programme.desc: A celebration of the life of former England goalkeeper Gordon Banks, who won the World Cup in 1966, and was also remembered for a sensational save from Pele in the tournament four years later. Featuring insights from past and present players, as well as Banks himself.(n)

programme.title: Euro 96 Relived
programme.desc: Netherlands v England. A chance to see the Group A encounter at Wembley Stadium. The Dutch side contained the likes of Dennis Bergkamp, Clarence Seedorf and Edwin van der Sar, but it was Teddy Sheringham and Alan Shearer who took centre stage as England produced one of their finest-ever performances. Bob Wilson presented the coverage alongside John Barnes and Jack Charlton.(n)

programme.title: Sudden Death (1995)
programme.desc: Terrorists seize control of an ice-hockey stadium in the middle of a match attended by the US vice president. Taking the politician hostage, they threaten to blow up the building at the final whistle - but a resourceful former fireman takes it upon himself to thwart their dastardly plot. Action thriller, starring Jean-Claude Van Damme, Powers Boothe, Raymond J Barry and Whittni Wright.(n)

programme.title: RoboCop (1987)

Sci-fi thriller, starring Peter Weller, Nancy Allen, Ronny Cox and Miguel Ferrer.(n)

programme.title: The Return of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: The mysterious disappearance of an aristocrat's butler, whose erudition seems strangely at odds with his lowly occupation, presents Holmes with a fresh mystery to solve. The key to the case lies in an ancient family ritual - and points the way to a historic treasure. Victorian detective drama, starring Jeremy Brett.(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur gives up smoking and drinking on doctor's orders, and soon appears to lose his grip on reality, demanding the police protect him from an apparently non-existent angry customer. George Cole and Gary Webster star.(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Doyle is shot by an intruder in his flat, and enters a near coma. Cowley fears a terrorist group - two of whose members were blown up during a recent car chase by CI5 - is responsible. While Doyle experiences surreal dreams involving a ghostly graveyard, Cowley and Bodie attempt to pursue the villain behind the attack. Action drama, with Martin Shaw, Lewis Collins and Gordon Jackson.(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: The Flying Squad foils the hijacking of a security truck before attempting to learn the identity of an informer. Colin Welland guest stars in the hard-hitting police drama, alongside John Thaw and Dennis Waterman.(n)

programme.title: Brian Moore Meets
programme.desc: A classic 1980 interview with former tennis star Bjorn Borg, as he prepared to defend his Wimbledon title for the fifth consecutive year. Cameras capture Borg's glamorous but exhausting lifestyle and also reveals how his upbringing helped to mould his ice-cold on-court persona.(n)

programme.title: Casino Royale (1967)
programme.desc: Ageing spy James Bond comes out of retirement to tackle a mysterious mastermind who wants to take over the world. Meanwhile, another agent disguises himself as Bond to face the evil genius over the baccarat table. Spoof spy adventure, starring David Niven, Peter Sellers, Woody Allen, Orson Welles, Deborah Kerr and Ursula Andress.(n)

With Sondra Locke, Bill McKinney and Pat Hingle.(n)

programme.sub-title: The Queen of Hearts Is Wild
programme.desc: A woman is the only witness to the murder of a police officer, but her gangster boyfriend is an old enemy of Theo, and tries to intimidate her into keeping quiet.(n)

programme.title: The Return of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: Captain Jack Crocker, a robust seafaring man, is implicated in the murder of beautiful aristocrat Lady Brackenstall's abusive husband. Drama, guest starring Oliver Tobias, with Jeremy Brett, Edward Hardwicke and Anne Louise Lambert.(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.sub-title: A Bird in the Hand Is Worth Two in Shepherd's Bush
programme.desc: While Arthur is away on business in Europe, Ray is given the task of guarding a valuable racing pigeon - but does not appear to have his mind on the job. Comedy drama, starring George Cole and Gary Webster.(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle are assigned to prompt Arab cultural attache and assassin Rahad into carrying out a murderous deed, in the hope they can catch him red-handed just before he commits the act and force him out of the country. Comedy actor John Junkin guests stars alongside Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins.(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: With Haskins in Toronto, his stand-in DCI Quirk (guest star Bill Maynard) becomes obsessed with catching a gang of thieves. When information is received suggesting the crooks are about to rob a jeweller's shop, officers are poised for action, but events take an unexpected turn as the informant seemingly has a score to settle with Quirk first. Hard-hitting police drama, with John Thaw and Dennis Waterman.(n)

programme.title: Brian Moore Meets
programme.desc: A classic 1981 interview with the golf legend, who was then at the peak of his powers at the age of 22. Moore travels to Seve's home town of Pedrana in Northern Spain to find out about his upbringing and examines his single-minded pursuit of excellence.(n)

programme.desc: James Bond's investigation into the death of a world-renowned scientist working on solar technology draws him into a battle of wits with a stealthy assassin, who dispatches his victims with a golden bullet and has the British secret agent next in his sights. Spy adventure, starring Roger Moore, Christopher Lee, Britt Ekland and Maud Adams.(n)

Unfortunately, completion of the task requires him to jeopardise a titled lady's reputation - something he is naturally reluctant to do. Victorian detective drama, starring Jeremy Brett.(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur spies a business opportunity as he and Ray prepare to pay their respects to the late Charlie Johnson, but the deceased's widow has other ideas. Comedy drama, starring George Cole and Gary Webster.(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: A notorious hard-nosed villain offers to co-operate with the police and gives himself up in return for protection for his wife and their children, so Bodie and Doyle are assigned to perform the task. Crime drama, starring Lewis Collins, Martin Shaw and Gordon Jackson.(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A series of robberies is committed on affluent couples travelling down a country lane, and it transpires two of the suspected offenders are wealthy themselves. Lesley-Anne Down guest stars alongside John Thaw and Dennis Waterman.(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: A racehorse owner is accused of killing both his champion thoroughbred and its vet, prompting old friend Quincy to track down the real culprit.(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: A nun seeks revenge on an airline boss she believes is responsible for her sister's death, and ends up in the middle of a dangerous battle of wills between Theo and the Mob.(n)

programme.title: The Return of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: The mysterious disappearance of an aristocrat's butler, whose erudition seems strangely at odds with his lowly occupation, presents Holmes with a fresh mystery to solve. The key to the case lies in an ancient family ritual - and points the way to a historic treasure. Victorian detective drama, starring Jeremy Brett.(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur gives up smoking and drinking on doctor's orders, and soon appears to lose his grip on reality, demanding the police protect him from an apparently non-existent angry customer. George Cole and Gary Webster star.(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Doyle is shot by an intruder in his flat, and enters a near coma. Cowley fears a terrorist group - two of whose members were blown up during a recent car chase by CI5 - is responsible. While Doyle experiences surreal dreams involving a ghostly graveyard, Cowley and Bodie attempt to pursue the villain behind the attack. Action drama, with Martin Shaw, Lewis Collins and Gordon Jackson.(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: The Flying Squad foils the hijacking of a security truck before attempting to learn the identity of an informer. Colin Welland guest stars in the hard-hitting police drama, alongside John Thaw and Dennis Waterman.(n)

programme.title: Brian Moore Meets
programme.desc: A classic 1980 interview with former tennis star Bjorn Borg, as he prepared to defend his Wimbledon title for the fifth consecutive year. Cameras capture Borg's glamorous but exhausting lifestyle and also reveals how his upbringing helped to mould his ice-cold on-court persona.(n)

programme.title: Casino Royale (1967)
programme.desc: Ageing spy James Bond comes out of retirement to tackle a mysterious mastermind who wants to take over the world. Meanwhile, another agent disguises himself as Bond to face the evil genius over the baccarat table. Spoof spy adventure, starring David Niven, Peter Sellers, Woody Allen, Orson Welles, Deborah Kerr and Ursula Andress.(n)

programme.desc: As the country prepares for the easing of lockdown, this documentary tells the stories of the elderly and vulnerable people self-isolating during the coronavirus crisis, for whom lockdown may continue for many more months. Featuring people from all over the nation and filmed on location, their stories convey the immense challenge of being especially vulnerable in the midst of a pandemic. Suzanne, who has terminal cancer, has come up with a novel approach to seeing her friends, while Desmond has been forced to move in with his daughter and granddaughter to avoid the virus, which has already taken the lives of at least four of his friends.(n)

programme.title: Walking with Elephants

Together with his guide Kane, Lev treks along the route taken by the male elephants through the immense salt pans of the Makgadikgadi, via the frontier town of Gweta, to the edge of the Okavango Delta. programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The competition comes from in and around Belfast, where the first host is chatty PR manager Claire. She aims to impress with a menu made up of her favourite restaurant dishes, but never having plucked a pheasant before, she has a challenge on her hands. The guests she's hoping to impress are DJ Ryan, joiner and part-time inventor Neil, charity events manager Cailin, and window cleaning manager Paul, on a lively night filled with laughter, music and dancing.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Carpenter Neil hosts the second dinner party in and around Belfast in a bid to win the £1000 prize, presenting good old fashioned, country style cuisine to his guests. With a farmyard full of animals joining in the fun, discussions ranging from disabled chickens to eating stinging nettles and a guest performance from Neil's very own creation, ''head in a jar', it's proves to be a weird and wonderful experience out in the countryside.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Debonair window cleaning business owner and keen golfer Paul has cut a dash already this week in and around Belfast with his sense of style, and now he hopes to impress with his fine food. For Ryan and Neil, most of the fancy fare is uncharted territory, and while Cailin and Claire are impressed with the fine dining, Paul sails close to the wind when he suggests that they might like to be his ladies-in-waiting.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

Danni Menzies has five options for their £150,000 budget, but whether the couple will be impressed enough to put in an offer remains to be seen.(n)

programme.title: Top Wing

programme.title: Sunny Bunnies
programme.desc: The bunnies discover a box with a helicopter building kit inside. Not bothering with the instructions, they get straight to work, only for their construction to collapse.(n)

programme.title: Jeremy Vine

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Ben Reeve from Lewisham Council's Rogue Landlord Unit aims to help Lola, who stopped paying rent 18 months earlier in an effort to get her negligent landlord to deal with her dilapidated flat's many problems. Eviction specialists also come to the aid of Amit, who found himself continuing his late father's battle with a nightmare renter, and 70-year-old landlady Joan, who started receiving unnerving threats from her rent-skipping tenant after she started legal proceedings to get him evicted.(n)

programme.title: 5 News at Lunchtime
programme.desc: Round-up of the day's headlines from around the world.(n)

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: Blanche is thrilled when her father announces he is getting married, until she realises that the bride-to-be is much younger than herself.(n)

programme.title: Home and Away

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: Toadie has a decision to make when Dee floats the idea of staying put, while Terese asks Ned to organise the relaunch of the rooftop pool, but Paul and Pierce have other ideas.(n)

programme.title: A Deadly Vendetta (2018)
programme.desc: A teenager with a promising soccer career ahead of her moves to a new school, and finds herself playing alongside girls who were once her bitterest rivals. She finds herself subjected to a campaign of harassment by the coach and her daughter, and must find a way to prove her story before her academic and sporting futures are destroyed. Thriller, starring Christina Cox and Sarah Grey.(n)

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Pregnant Rachel considers moving out, prompting Ross to tell her about a vacancy in his building, and Joey creates a nursery to persuade her to stay. Eric dumps Ursula for twin sister Phoebe, and Monica suspects her new maid could be stealing her jeans - among other things. Guest starring Sean Penn.(n)

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Rachel finally tells her father she is pregnant, prompting him to confront Ross - who is just snuggling up to new girlfriend Mona. Monica feels guilty about denying Chandler a stripper on his stag night so she books one to make amends - but he gets a lot more than he bargained for.(n)

programme.title: 5 News at 5
programme.desc: Round-up of the day's headlines from around the world.(n)

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: Toadie has a decision to make when Dee floats the idea of staying put, while Terese asks Ned to organise the relaunch of the rooftop pool, but Paul and Pierce have other ideas.(n)

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Maggie realises it is time to stand up to Marco, while Ryder plays match-maker as Nikau and Bella tiptoe cautiously around each other. John begins to see the error of his ways.(n)

programme.title: 5 News Tonight
programme.desc: A round-up of the evening's headlines, including coverage of the latest national and international stories.(n)

programme.title: Wonderful World of Chocolate
programme.desc: Documentary offering a glimpse inside the confectionery world, visiting factories, chocolatiers and shops, and even revealing the Queen's favourite chocolates.(n)

programme.title: Drain the Oceans
programme.sub-title: Hitler's Invisible U-Boat: Draining the Ocean
programme.desc: Marine archaeologists and historians go in search of Hitler's secrets, hidden deep underwater, revealing how the Nazis waged a secret war across the world's oceans. This documentary uses CGI graphics to drain the oceans and investigate the lost wrecks of the Second World War.(n)

programme.title: The World's Most Scenic Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Beginning in the mountaintop town of Creel, the Chihuahua Pacifico Express - or El Chepe for short - winds its way through the Tarahumara Mountains, home to the reclusive Raramuri people. Divisadero, Mexico's most panoramic station, offers the first glimpse of Copper Canyon. Created by six separate yet conjoined gorges, it is four times the size of the Grand Canyon. The train continues on to the Urique Canyon, which at 6,200ft deep it is the deepest in North America. The final leg crosses the state of Sinaloa, Mexico's breadbasket, before reaching Los Mochis and the gateway to the Pacific Ocean. Narrated by Bill Nighy.(n)

programme.title: Portillo's Empire Journey
programme.desc: Michael Portillo visits Jamaica, the Caribbean capital of the Empire, heading to Fort Charles where the Empire enlisted the help of pirates to defend the new territory from the Spanish. At the old naval headquarters, Michael learns how the Royal Navy moved in to replace the pirates as Britain struck a deal with Spain to keep her territories. The island needed a new source of wealth, and found it in the triangular trade as slaves were shipped from Africa to work sugar plantations. In Pinnacle, birthplace of the Rastafari movement, Michael learns how Jamaicans were later inspired to create a new religion.(n)

programme.title: When... Goes Horribly Wrong
programme.sub-title: When Comedy Shows Go Horribly Wrong
programme.desc: Julian Clary narrates a compilation of disastrous mishaps from comedy shows. Featuring a stunt fight that ended badly for comedian Russell Howard, an incident that left Tony Hancock determined to never perform live again, and a rare outtake from Tom, Dick and Harriet. That long-forgotten sitcom from 1982 and 1983 featured former Return of the Saint star Ian Ogilvy and actor/director Lionel Jeffries. Their stunt involving a partly submerged car could have ended in disaster.(n)

programme.title: The Professionals (1966)
programme.desc: A wealthy Texan hires four mercenaries to rescue his wife, who has been abducted and taken to Mexico by a bandit gang. However, when they find her, she refuses to leave and they have to take her by force - ending in a cross-border chase with the outlaws on their trail. Western adventure, with Burt Lancaster, Lee Marvin, Robert Ryan, Woody Strode, Jack Palance and Claudia Cardinale.(n)

programme.title: How Green Was My Valley (1941)
programme.desc: Residents of a Welsh mining community at the start of the 20th century struggle to come to terms with the decline in their traditional way of life. Oscar-winning drama, starring Walter Pidgeon, Maureen O'Hara, Donald Crisp and Roddy McDowall.(n)

programme.title: The Martian Interview Special
programme.desc: Director Ridley Scott and stars Kate Mara and Chiwetel Ejiofor talk about making the 2015 sci-fi adventure, which stars Matt Damon as an astronaut stranded on Mars.(n)

programme.title: Gallipoli (1981)
programme.desc: Two patriotic Australians join the army to fight during the First World War. However, their youthful idealism is quickly eroded by the harsh reality of the conflict, and their friendship is tested to the very limits of endurance as they prepare for combat ahead of the pivotal battle of Gallipoli in Turkey. Drama, directed by Peter Weir, starring Mel Gibson, Mark Lee, Bill Kerr and David Argue.(n)

programme.title: A Beautiful Mind (2001)
programme.desc: Biopic of the brilliant American mathematician John Nash, whose prospects of a promising career with the CIA were confounded by paranoid delusions and schizophrenia as the stress of the job proved too much of a burden. Drama, starring Russell Crowe, Jennifer Connelly, Ed Harris, Judd Hirsch, Paul Bettany and Christopher Plummer.(n)

programme.title: The Martian Interview Special
programme.desc: Director Ridley Scott and stars Kate Mara and Chiwetel Ejiofor talk about making the 2015 sci-fi adventure, which stars Matt Damon as an astronaut stranded on Mars.(n)

programme.title: The Martian (2015)
programme.desc: A manned mission to Mars is abruptly abandoned, with one crew member left for dead. However, the astronaut has survived and faces a series of challenges - to contact Earth and arrange his rescue and to survive for the years it will take for a spacecraft to reach him on resources intended to last the crew a month. Ridley Scott's sci-fi drama based on the novel by Andy Weir, starring Matt Damon, Jessica Chastain, Kristen Wiig and Chiwetel Ejiofor.(n)

programme.title: The Daughter (2015)
programme.desc: A man returning home for his father's wedding uncovers a secret that threatens to destroy the lives of those he left behind. Drama, with Paul Schneider and Geoffrey Rush.(n)

programme.title: The Possibilities Are Endless (2014)
programme.desc: Documentary following singer-songwriter Edwyn Collins' rehabilitation after he suffered two cerebral haemorrhages that affected his memory, weakened his right side and left him able to say four things - including the phrase 'the possibilities are endless'. Edward Lovelace and James Hall's film tracks the former Orange Juice frontman as he and his wife Grace Maxwell slowly put his life back together and come to terms with the dramatic consequences of the haemorrhages.(n)

programme.title: The Naked Truth (1957)
programme.desc: Celebrities threatened with blackmail decide they would rather murder the extortionist than pay him, but things do not go according to plan. Comedy, starring Terry-Thomas, Peter Sellers, Dennis Price, Peggy Mount, Shirley Eaton and Joan Sims.(n)

programme.title: The Spy in Black (1939)
programme.desc: A German spy falls for an apparently sympathetic schoolteacher during a First World War mission, unaware the object of his affections is in fact an English double agent, a situation that threatens to jeopardise an operation to destroy the British fleet in the Orkneys in 1917. Powell and Pressburger's romantic drama, starring Conrad Veidt, Valerie Hobson and Marius Goring.(n)

programme.title: The Man in the Iron Mask (1977)
programme.desc: The despotic king of France imprisons his identical twin and sees to it that he always wears a metal mask to avoid being identified, but musketeer D'Artagnan has a plan to restore the rightful heir to the throne. Swashbuckling adventure, based on the classic story by Alexandre Dumas, starring Richard Chamberlain in a dual role, with Patrick McGoohan, Louis Jourdan and Jenny Agutter.(n)

programme.title: Three Violent People (1956)
programme.desc: A tough rancher falls for a woman he encounters at the end of the American Civil War, and promptly sweeps her off her feet. However, the couple's attempts to settle into married life are threatened by dark secrets in her past, as well as rising tensions both within the family, and on the political scene. Western, starring Charlton Heston, Anne Baxter, Gilbert Roland and Tom Tryon.(n)

programme.title: Cowboys & Aliens (2011)
programme.desc: A Wild-West town dominated by a cattle baron comes under attack by invaders from another world. It falls to a mysterious stranger with no memory to unite the townsfolk, a gang of outlaws and an Apache tribe, in a bid to fight back against the aliens, and learn the truth about his own past. Sci-fi Western, with Daniel Craig, Harrison Ford, Olivia Wilde and Sam Rockwell.(n)

programme.title: A Walk in the Woods (2015)
programme.desc: A travel writer decides to hike the Appalachian Trail, a 2,200-mile journey through some of America's most rugged terrain. However, the only companion he can find for his exhibition is a roguish old friend, who sees it as an opportunity to get out of paying his debts. Fact-based drama adapted from Bill Bryson's travel book, starring Robert Redford, Nick Nolte and Emma Thompson.(n)

programme.title: The Family (2013)
programme.desc: A gangster testifies against his partners in crime, before he and his family are relocated to rural France under the witness protection program. Despite their FBI handler's efforts to keep them under control, they soon return to their criminal ways - while the mobsters they betrayed are soon on their trail. Comedy thriller, with Robert De Niro, Michelle Pfeiffer and Tommy Lee Jones.(n)

programme.title: Child 44 (2015)
programme.desc: A secret police agent in 1950s Russia is disgraced for refusing to denounce his own wife as a traitor. Reassigned to a remote outpost, he tries to bring a serial child murderer to justice, but faces an official cover-up by a rival, who wants to maintain the illusion that Soviet society is without crime. Detective thriller based on Tom Rob Smith's novel, starring Tom Hardy, Gary Oldman and Noomi Rapace.(n)

programme.title: Sorry, Wrong Number (1948)
programme.desc: Rich, neurotic Leona Stevenson lies bedridden at home and is trying to phone her husband. But the lines are crossed, and she accidentally overhears a call in which two men are planning a murder. She frantically tries to raise the alarm, and when at last she manages to convince the telephone company of her story, she comes to realise with increasing horror who their intended victim is. Thriller, starring Barbara Stanwyck, Burt Lancaster, Ann Richards and Wendell Corey.(n)

programme.title: Destroyer (1943)
programme.desc: A former welder working on the Navy destroyer whose construction he oversaw angers his crewmates with his relentless quest for perfection. Wartime adventure, starring Edward G Robinson, Glenn Ford, Marguerite Chapman and Regis Toomey.(n)

programme.title: The Malta Story (1953)
programme.desc: The island of Malta comes under siege during the Second World War, as both Allied and Axis forces see it as vital to their supply lines. A British reconnaissance pilot arrives on the island, and plays a crucial role in defending it from the advancing enemy forces. Drama, starring Alec Guinness, Anthony Steel, Jack Hawkins, Muriel Pavlow and Flora Robson.(n)

programme.title: 40 Guns to Apache Pass (1967)
programme.desc: A cavalry captain sets out to deliver a shipment of rifles to a fort besieged by marauding Apaches, but the mission is undermined by a villainous corporal who plans to steal the weapons and sell them to the enemy. Western, starring Audie Murphy, Michael Burns, Laraine Stephens, Kenneth Tobey and Michael Keep.(n)

programme.title: Home Alone 3 (1997)
programme.desc: Cheeky eight-year-old Alex Pruitt is delighted to be given a remote-controlled toy car by his neighbour - unaware it contains a stolen microchip. A gang of crooks heads to Alex's town with the intention of recovering the valuable item, but the resourceful youngster has prepared for their arrival with a series of nasty booby-traps. Comedy sequel, starring Alex D Linz, Olek Krupa, Rya Kihlstedt and Lenny von Dohlen.(n)

programme.title: Heat (1995)
programme.desc: An armoured car hold-up sets a veteran police detective on the trail of a highly intelligent career criminal, whom he is obsessed with capturing. Meanwhile, the robber is planning an elaborate bank heist as his final job before retiring, and a complex game of cat and mouse develops between the two. Michael Mann's crime thriller, starring Al Pacino, Robert De Niro, Val Kilmer, Jon Voight, Tom Sizemore and Natalie Portman.(n)

programme.title: Rules of Engagement
programme.desc: A resentful employee files a sexual harassment claim against Audrey, while Timmy challenges Jeff to a game of cricket after being teased for playing the sport.(n)

programme.title: Rules of Engagement
programme.desc: Adam and Jennifer plan a secret wedding, but a series of unfortunate events places it in jeopardy and they have to lie to Russell and Timmy to stop them finding out about the ceremony. Comedy, starring David Spade and Oliver Hudson.(n)

Meanwhile, Riley worries that Dr Amy Shaw is trying to change Danny when he turns his back on their traditional 'Halfaween' costume party. Comedy, starring Jean-Luc Bilodeau, and guest starring Grace Phipps and Lacey Chabert.(n)

programme.desc: Stuart hosts a Halloween party at the comic-book store, and Howard talks incessantly about his work on board the International Space Station, annoying his wife and friends. Penny and Leonard have a steamy encounter at the lab. Guest starring real-life astronaut Buzz Aldrin.(n)

Meanwhile, Lennox and Zander avoid discussing their feelings for each other. Starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Mel thinks she might be pregnant, but is unsure how to tell Joe, who is in the middle of preparing for an important business pitch. Meanwhile, Ryder forms a study group with an eclectic mix of people, and Lennox and Zander get their wires crossed.(n)

programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Ben learns that is it all his fault that Riley does not like celebrating her birthday. He plans a surprise to make it up to her but, in typical fashion, disaster ensues. Meanwhile, Bonnie reveals that she forgot to complete some paperwork and that Ben isn't Emma's sole custodian. Starring Jean-Luc Bilodeau.(n)

programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Ben and Danny wear elf suits on their quest to find a particular Christmas gift for Emma, while Riley and Tucker help Bonnie plan a seasonal celebration. Comedy, starring Jean-Luc Bilodeau and Tahj Mowry.(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Peralta tries to make a deal with a suspect Diaz has just arrested in an attempt to catch a car thief he has been tracking for years. programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Peralta and Santiago's ongoing bet about who can make the most arrests comes to an embarrassing conclusion, while Boyle receives a medal following the shooting, but his pain medication loosens his tongue and he starts to reveal what he really thinks of his colleagues.(n)

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Barry seeks fresh inspiration when he loses his standing as a champion athlete at school and attempts to introduce a new sport to his friends. Murray refuses to let Beverly help out at the furniture shop when sales take a nosedive, but she's convinced the latest home furnishing craze holds the answer.(n)

Barry lobbies for the dance to be based on his favourite movie Footloose, while Adam and Pops encourage Murray to learn to dance in order to make Beverly happy.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Amy is furious when she learns that Sheldon has shared intimate details of their relationship with his colleagues at the university, prompting their first fight since moving in together. Raj considers trying to live without financial support from his father, who thinks he is spoilt.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Cut off from his father's fortune, Raj struggles to balance his books, so decides to appoint Sheldon to organise his finances and get him out of debt. Penny considers joining the guys at Comic-Con because she thinks Leonard wants her to come along.(n)

programme.title: Hollyoaks Favourites
programme.desc: Former Hollyoaks star Matt Littler relives a classic episode. As Max's fears for his health grow, Clare continues her plan to kill her husband.(n)

programme.title: Zoey's Extraordinary Playlist

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: Naked Attraction

In the first edition, Aina, a music producer from London, wants to find someone adventurous who she can have a deeper connection with, while Guildford-based designer Mal - who has dated men and women - is curious to see who she will be drawn to when all options are laid bare before her.(n)

programme.title: Gogglebox

programme.desc: Sabrina's disruptive cousin Amanda is sent to Witchright Hall, a special school where magically challenged witches are taught to use their powers constructively in the mortal world. Melissa Joan Hart and Emily Hart star.(n)

Family comedy, starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence.(n)

Comedy, starring Jean-Luc Bilodeau, with guest star Caroline Rhea.(n)

programme.title: Baby Daddy

Ian and Cathy McNeill take their guests trout fishing, and round the day off with a meal in their own restaurant with entertainment from a traditional tenor. However, the next morning is not so harmonious as the guests jostle for access to the only available shower.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition moves to Bournemouth, where owners John and Chris Griffith welcome their guests to the Bed & Breakfast by the Beach. The visit gets off to a bad start when hyper-critical Jill is reduced to tears by the discovery that her room does not have a smoke alarm, and the tension continues to mount at dinner, where the hosts regale their guests with racy tales.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed

However, the outlandish plans - including Jacuzzis in the battlements and water-spouting gargoyles - have Kevin worried the project could become a garish nightmare, and his scepticism increases when he realises Sean has not consulted an architect. Disaster seems to be looming as the contractors grow frustrated and planning regulations are ignored, but somehow things slowly begin to take shape - until a national economic crisis plunges everything into chaos.(n)

programme.desc: Phillip aims to transform three derelict rooms at Chateau de la Fare into the perfect bridal suite for his daughter Penny's wedding in three months' time.(n)

programme.desc: Charlie Luxton meets a self-building couple in Caterham, Surrey, who have spotted the huge potential of a dilapidated bungalow, which they plan to reimagine as a split-level home.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at the contemporary Fraoch House in Edinburgh, where Gareth Houston and his mother Heather Davidson hope to impress the guests with their stylish interiors and modern approach to hospitality.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Newlywed B&B owners Kevin and Elaine invite their rivals to a traditional establishment in Marple, Greater Manchester, hoping its quaint location will be enough to secure the prize. However, the guests soon make it abundantly clear that it will take more than an idyllic setting to fulfil their expectations.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Suzy Way, the owner of a modern and unconventional B&B near Loughborough, Leicestershire, and her sister Niki Brown prepare an activity-packed day featuring water sport wakeboarding and a home-made curry to win their guests over.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: John Benson is the last to host the group at his B&B in Braintree, Essex, where he hopes his strong business sense and capable manager will make a good impression. A day out at a skidpan track is not to everyone's liking, but dinner is a more relaxed affair, enhanced by spooky stories and a sing-song.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The four sets of owners discover what the other competitors think of their businesses by opening the cash-filled envelopes, which contain what their rivals believe their hosts should receive. They meet for one last time to learn who has won the best value B&B of the week.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Max McMurdo travel the length of Britain seeking items to fix up, flog on and sell for profit. Max upcycles a brass propeller into a stylish table lamp, while Henry goes all Victorian with an old pond yacht.(n)

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: Timothy West and Prunella Scales visit the Burgundy region of France, where they travel along the Canal du Nivernais, which passes through towns and villages steeped in history. The duo also visit the market town of Chablis to help with the annual grape harvest and the medieval city of Auxerre before reflecting on what the journey has meant to them as a married couple.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Phillip and Janet call on friends and family to help complete their bridal suite in time for their daughter's wedding, and Mariam and Johnno await the arrival of seven guests.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets a pair of civil-partnered university professors in Hertfordshire who have decided to build their first home from scratch. In addition to all the complications this usually entails, Celia Brackenridge and Diana Woodward have also enlisted the services of industrial designers who have developed a new computer-assisted method for cutting precision-engineered building blocks from scratch. However, though the technique may be clever, its creators have never created a building before - meaning everyone involved with the project is taking a giant leap into the unknown.(n)

programme.title: Outlander
programme.desc: After Jamie refused his aunt's plantation, he and Claire set out into the North Carolina wilderness in search of a new place to call home. In the 20th century, Brianna and Roger's romance heats up during a road trip.(n)

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy words-and-numbers quiz, with David Mitchell and Cariad Lloyd taking on Jon Richardson and Russell Howard. Sam Simmons joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters.(n)

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: Dougal and Ted get involved in yet another bout of rivalry with their counterparts over on Rugged Island - this time by writing a song that will allow them the dubious honour of representing Ireland in the Eurovision-style music contest Eurosong. Ardal O'Hanlon and Dermot Morgan star.(n)

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: Bishop Brennan descends on Craggy Island to investigate rumours that Father Jack has developed the unfortunate habit of sleepwalking in the nude - only to come face to face with his worst fear. Clerical comedy, with Ardal O'Hanlon and Dermot Morgan.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 61-year-old woman is airlifted to hospital after a car mounted the pavement and ran her over and doctors quickly get her into the CT scanner to ascertain the full extent of her injuries. Elsewhere, 91-year-old Sam arrives after suffering a suspected stroke, and four-month-old boy Teddy is rushed in after falling face first out of his car seat, hitting the pavement.(n)

programme.title: Shocking Emergency Calls
programme.desc: First-hand accounts of deadly wildfires in Paradise, California, that wiped out homes, towns and lives, telling the stories of the emergency responders and those who escaped.(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped

programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp joins jewellery-maker Rachel Jeffrey to help a soon-to-be-married couple create their own wedding rings, and learns how gold can revive an old picture frame at little cost.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: Glamorgan-based couple Richard and Felicity Arcelli want a second home in the Coimbra region of Portugal and are looking to spend £40,000 on a renovation project that builder Richard can turn into a permanent home. Can presenter Sara Damergi find them their dream property?(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien arrive in York, where Henry struggles to squeeze into some motorcycle leathers and Simon gets into deep water when he tries to restore an aluminium boat.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit a stately home in Somerset, where Simon is convinced he has uncovered a genuine Tudor relic.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Phillip and Janet call on friends and family as they're down to the wire trying to finish the bridal suite in time for their daughter Penny's wedding. Mariam and Johnno get to work preparing all their bedrooms as they're due to host seven guests in three weeks' time. It's far from easy as Mariam struggles to find enough material to make curtains and finds out that a custom-made mattress doesn't fit an antique bed. Emma and Paul complete their Charles Rennie Mackintosh-themed bedroom in time for Emma's brother George's visit to celebrate his 60th birthday.(n)

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton returns to East Sussex to catch up with Steve and with an eco-efficient, straw-baled self-built home set in a woodland glade. After a long and testing process, Charlie's on-site as this incredible project finally reaches the finishing line.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Claire Hilton, who runs Robertsbrook Guest House in Swanage, Dorset, feels she has a good chance of winning the title of best B&B as another round of the competition gets under way. However, guests Lynn Self and Ziggy Broome have doubts over the cleanliness of their bedroom, and another couple are accustomed to more opulent surroundings.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Lynn Self and Ziggy Broome hope to impress with their two-star B&B Eden Lodge in Falmouth, Cornwall. However, the state of their property disappoints their fellow competitors. At dinner, James and Maria Church reveal the truth about their famous daughter Charlotte, and the following morning's breakfast reduces some of the guests to fits of giggles.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: It is the turn of Charlotte Church's parents James and Maria to play host at Dexby Townhouse in Cardiff, and they pull out all the stops by laying on a day of white-water rafting and a magic performance courtesy of their nephew Elliot. But the following morning, some of the guests are kept waiting for breakfast.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Newlyweds Pip Easby and Nicky Lesser-Easby, who run Muddifords Court Country House in Devon, open their doors for the final stay of the competition. The Churches find their four-poster bed too small, and not all the guests enjoy their trip to a local glass-blowing factory, but feedback at check-out time leaves the hosts feeling confident.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to discover how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers and to air their grievances. An underpayment from a surprising source changes the dynamics of the group and throws the competition wide open, before the winner is announced.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien travel Britain looking for items to fix up and flog on behalf of their owners. In this episode they're close to the Malvern Hills. Henry picks two saddles from horse owner Sabrina and converts them into bar stools, while Simon makes a handsome profit on a 1960s kitchen cupboard.(n)

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: Built nearly a century before the British canal system, the Canal du Midi in the south of France has been designated a world heritage site. Timothy West and Prunella Scales explore a picturesque stretch, as is it meanders toward the Mediterranean. They visit the town of Beziers and the Malpas Tunnel - Europe's first navigable canal tunnel - and taste the delights of lamb's brain fritters, as well as brandy that is almost 200 years old.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Emma and Paul prepare for their biggest chateau booking ever as they host American veterans for the 75th anniversary of D-Day. Mariam and Johnno transform their chateau's grand salon by installing reproduction Parquet de Versailles flooring and hand-painted murals. Plus, Karen and Paul aim to transform the old stable and coach house into a space fit for hosting weddings.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Mary Martin and Carl Turner want to build a home that resembles a giant stack of glass cubes in Brixton, south London. The couple are hoping the structure will become an instant landmark, while on the inside they are planning a Zen retreat. But to succeed, their finances must be pushed to their limit, so Carl takes on as many of the specialist jobs as he can to save money. Kevin McCloud follows their progress.(n)

programme.title: Thou Shalt Not Kill
programme.desc: The death of a psychologist who assesses the suitability of soldiers for special combat missions causes a great stir. Valeria and the team are presented with more than one suspect, including the wife of a possible lover and a couple of soldiers involved in drug-running. The members of the homicide unit are tasked with sifting through conflicting stories and motives to get at the real killer while circumventing interference aimed at them from the higher echelons of the military. Crime drama, with Miriam Leone. In Italian.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The staff at St George's A&E in south-west London tend to 59-year-old Debby, who cut her hand on her lawnmower while distracted by thoughts of her son's forthcoming wedding. Also visiting the emergency room is 70-year-old George, whose eye has been impaled by a piece of driftwood, after he fell while bailing rainwater from his boat moored on the Thames. Plus, eight-year-old Phoebe arrives after accidentally sitting on a pair of scissors she left on a chair - getting them stuck in her thigh.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The documentary charting the work of staff at St George's A&E continues, this time focusing on the stories of patients whose friendships have seen them through good times and bad. Phil, a 60-year-old bricklayer, is rushed in after suffering multiple injuries following a short fall from a ladder at work, and his business partner Paul discusses how the pair bonded over the course of 28 years. Meanwhile, 93-year-old widow Violet arrives after fracturing her leg while preparing to go out for lunch with the friends who have supported her since the death of her husband, and 18-year-old dance student Shiana comes to A&E with her mother and her best friend, after injuring her ankle during a rehearsal.(n)

programme.desc: Mock the Week chairman Dara O Briain comperes the comedy show at London's Hammersmith Apollo. He warms up the audience before introducing routines by stand-up stars Felicity Ward and Mo Gilligan.(n)

programme.title: Big Zuu's Big Eats
programme.desc: Grime artist and chef Big Zuu is off to Portsmouth to cook the perfect post-performance meal for comedian Rosie Jones, including fresh fish and chips just like her Nanna made.(n)

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Asim Chaudhry has fun with drainpipes, and Tim Vine gets stuck in a tunnel, and Greg Davies gives Alex Horne a sharp telling off.(n)

programme.title: Judge Romesh
programme.desc: A spurned spouse tries to get access to his dog-obsessed wife, and a sword swallower tries to resolve his issues with a blindfolded fire juggler.(n)

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.sub-title: Walking Through History: Norman Conquest of Pembrokeshire
programme.desc: Tony Robinson heads to the south-west corner of Wales and one of Britain's finest coastal paths to find out why Pembrokeshire feels so English, even though it's more than 100 miles from the border. With its numerous castles and a fine cathedral, there are sure signs the Normans stamped their mark over the area. Tony's four-day walk traces the story of their conquest of the county, which he discovers was very different from the knockout victory at Hastings, involving a long, fiercely fought struggle.(n)

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.sub-title: Walking Through History: Nazi Occupation - Channel Islands
programme.desc: Tony Robinson heads to Guernsey and Jersey to tell the story of the islands' surrender to the Nazis during the Second World War. In Guernsey, he hears of the first bombing raid that heralded the German troops' arrival and sees the spot where the first-ever British commando landed in secret, only to see the planned revenge raid fail spectacularly. A ferry then takes him on to Jersey, where he finds almost all of its beaches have a bunker or a machine gun post left over from the conflict.(n)

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.sub-title: The Secrets of the Murder Bureau
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston investigates a secret department of the Bulgarian government charged with carrying out assassinations in nine other countries during the Cold War.(n)

programme.title: Nazi Hunters
programme.desc: The search for Josef Mengele, one of the most wanted Nazi war criminals, which was led by Holocaust survivor Simon Wiesenthal.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The Antiques Roadshow pays tribute to Her Majesty the Queen as she becomes Britain's longest-serving monarch.(n)

programme.title: Impossible Railways
programme.desc: Since their meteoric rise in the 19th Century, railways have been at the forefront of modern engineering. They've conquered dizzying altitudes, wilderness, seas and cities.(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A settlement in Argentina destroyed by floods, a town in the US that has been erased, and a Soviet dream-town that turned into a nuclear nightmare.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents an episode from RAF Coningsby, an operational air base in Lincolnshire, where she takes to the sky in a Dakota aircraft and examines some air force memorabilia including a teddy bear that was on board one of the Dambuster planes and designs for the Vulcan bomber. Items brought in to be valued include a painting by John Singer Sergeant, a crystal decanter and an officer's coat from the American Civil War.(n)

programme.title: Ganges
programme.desc: Exploring the story of the river, following its course from the Himalayas to their foothills. The programme reveals its enormous influence on the day-to-day living of a continent, how it has shaped Indian culture and religion and why it attracts a vast array of wildlife including snow leopards, Asiatic brown bears and bearded vultures.(n)

programme.title: The Life of Mammals
programme.desc: David Attenborough uses advanced surveillance technology to observe the feeding habits of herbivores, including elephants forced to undertake epic migrations to ensure a steady diet of the plants they need to survive. He also considers the habits of the sloth, which takes a month to digest each meal, and reveals scientific research suggesting that grazing wildebeest do not follow the rains as originally thought, but detect trails of phosphorus in the grass.(n)

programme.title: Impossible Railways
programme.desc: Since their rise in the 19th century, railways have been at the forefront of modern engineering and transport, conquering dizzying altitudes, wilderness, seas and cities.(n)

programme.title: Nazi Hunters
programme.desc: The Jewish group the Avengers who, as the Second World War was ending, hunted down persecutors known to have contributed to deaths during the Holocaust, including Wilhelm Stuckart.(n)

programme.title: War Factories
programme.desc: The role played by engineering giant Vickers during the Second World War, including the development of the Spitfire and how the Royal Navy recovered from losing so many warships.(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A seemingly endless concrete line stretching through the French countryside, which sits as a monument to a discarded engineering advancement.(n)

programme.title: Shipwreck Secrets
programme.desc: The discovery of a wreck off the coast of Florida sends a team of underwater explorers on a mission to discover the identity of a ship that simply shouldn't exist.(n)

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: Marlene allows Boycie to buy a maximum of six cows for the farm - and is less than impressed when Elgin, Jed and Bryan turn up with a herd of 300. The vet is called out when Rocky the bull fails to take any notice of the females, and Tyler prepares to sit a local private school's entrance exam. John Challis and Sue Holderness star.(n)

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: Boycie struggles to come to terms with the news that his prize bull is gay, and Marlene finds she is missing the noise of the city after suffering sleepless nights. Tyler is delighted to learn he has failed all but two of his GCSEs, thinking he will no longer be accepted at a private school - but his parents have other ideas. John Challis and Sue Holderness star.(n)

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: Marlene discovers the farmhands concocting a virility potion for Rocky the bull, and manages to sneak a dose into Boycie's coffee. Make-up is found in Tyler's room, and his parents are relieved to be told he has a girlfriend - until it transpires she is also his teacher. John Challis, Sue Holderness and Jack Doolan star.(n)

programme.desc: Capt Blackadder applies to join the Royal Flying Corps, seeing it as a great way of getting out of the trenches and into a classroom. Comedy, guest starring Rik Mayall and Adrian Edmondson, with Rowan Atkinson and Tony Robinson.(n)

programme.title: Blackadder

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.sub-title: Walking Through History: King John's Ruin - Peak District
programme.desc: As the 800th anniversary of the signing of Magna Carta in June 1215 approaches, Tony Robinson sets out to discover the real King John. His walk across the medieval monarch's stamping grounds of Sherwood Forest and the Peak District reveals a hapless man stumbling from crisis to crisis who, in the end, had no choice but to sign away some of his power. In the midst of what are now cultivated fields, Tony finds the scant remains of Laxton Castle, once the control centre where strict 'forest law' was administered and where taxes and fines flooded into the royal coffers. Later, in Sherwood, he explores how the myth of Robin Hood has helped to skew the public's image of King John.(n)

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson hikes up from Lyme Regis on Dorset's Jurassic Coast to the Somerset Levels in the footsteps of a ragtag rebel army led by the young Duke of Monmouth, the illegitimate son of Charles II. He sailed in from Holland around Lyme's famous Cobb with a handful of soldiers, believing the people would support him in overthrowing the newly crowned James II, who was also Monmouth's uncle, but, more importantly, Roman Catholic. The year was 1685 and the country was riven by anti-Catholic feeling mixed up with revolutionary zeal. Monmouth marched through the fiercely independent towns of Devon, gathering support from artisans and farmworkers. Meeting up with Billy Bragg in Colyton, Tony learns about the kind of people prepared to risk their lives for the Monmouth Rebellion.(n)

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: The life of Mary Magdalene, a key figure in Christianity, having witnessed Jesus' death and resurrection.(n)

programme.title: Ganges
programme.desc: How the Gangetic Plains, once a wilderness teeming with wildlife, have been transformed into one of the most densely populated places on Earth - and how the region's tigers, lions, cheetahs and other species have been affected by human encroachment on their habitat.(n)

programme.title: The Life of Mammals
programme.desc: David Attenborough examines the world of rodents, whose constantly growing incisor teeth are used to chisel through any obstacle and range from squirrels and the English harvest mouse to the large South American capybara. He looks at examples of their dental prowess, showing how agoutis in Panama break open rock-hard nutshells and the skills employed by beavers in Wyoming as they fell massive trees to build dams rivalling feats of human engineering.(n)

programme.title: Ganges
programme.desc: The final leg of the river's journey sees it join its tributary the Brahmaputra. As the waters merge, they overspill their banks to fan out into hundreds of smaller rivulets and create the world's largest delta. The programme reveals how people and wildlife survive the inhospitable habitat, perhaps the last place on Earth where the Bengal tiger is still king.(n)

programme.title: The Life of Mammals
programme.desc: David Attenborough examines the hunting tactics of the world's carnivores. Arctic foxes in the frozen north must collect food during warmer times to survive the winter, while the big cats of the southern hemisphere rely on stealth and surprise to catch each meal. The presenter also reveals how leopards and tigers are solitary hunters, coming together only to mate, whereas lions stay in family groups so they can tackle larger prey and better protect their young.(n)

programme.title: The Plane Crash
programme.desc: Scientists, pilots and aviation experts participate in a staged aeroplane crash in a remote area of Mexican desert, hoping the exercise will reveal more about what happens to aircraft and their occupants during forced emergency landings. Pilot James 'JimBob' Slocum will put a Boeing 727 passenger jet on a crash course, parachuting out minutes before impact - with former US Navy pilot Leland 'Chip' Shanle then taking charge of the craft via remote control from a chase plane. After the crash, experts will analyse the site, along with data collected from cameras, instruments and hi-tech crash test dummies, to learn more about what happens when planes are forced out of the sky.(n)

programme.desc: As the country prepares for the easing of lockdown, this documentary tells the stories of the elderly and vulnerable people self-isolating during the coronavirus crisis, for whom lockdown may continue for many more months. Featuring people from all over the nation and filmed on location, their stories convey the immense challenge of being especially vulnerable in the midst of a pandemic. programme.title: Benefits Britain: Life on the Dole
programme.desc: The documentary examining the lives of unemployed people visits Hull, which has one of the highest unemployment rates and lowest average weekly wages in the UK. Rebecca is 16 and expecting her first baby with 17-year-old boyfriend Sonny, who is adamant he will be in work by the time the child arrives, while her foster mother Anita is concerned she will be unable to afford her house when her children move out. Gordon has not worked in decades and has very strong political views as to why employment has eluded him and many others in the north of England.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Agents Stewart McCracken and Elmore Victor head to Liverpool for what they expect to be a routine repossession, but instead face the most challenging eviction of their careers. Behind the front door, they find a critically ill two-year-old child with major heart problems, who is on constant life support and unable to breathe without a ventilator. Meanwhile, Brian O'Shaughnessy and Del Anglin chase £2,000 worth of debt owed by a business based in Kent, and recover £4,000 from a recycling business in Buckinghamshire. Plus, Stewart and Elmore encounter another family in crisis in Birmingham, where a retired couple who acted as guarantor for their daughter's tenancy are liable to pay over £3,000 after she defaulted.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!

programme.title: Motorway Patrol
programme.desc: Documentary following members of the New Zealand highway patrol as they witness some of the best and worst behaviour on the nation's motorways. The series is centred around the roads of Auckland, the country's largest urban area, and features motoring chaos including major traffic collisions, escaped animals and pedestrians on busy roads. There is also footage of drivers falling asleep at the wheel, overloaded vehicles and suspects caught with illegal substances - plus a host of excuses as the people pulled over try to persuade officers they have not done anything wrong.(n)

programme.sub-title: Horizon: The Secret World of Pain
programme.desc: The latest research into one of the most mysterious and common human experiences - pain. Breakthroughs have come from studying Jonathan Metz, who cut off his own arm to survive after it became lodged in a furnace, and three generations of an Italian family who do not feel extreme temperatures. programme.title: Strike It Lucky
programme.desc: Michael Barrymore hosts the madcap game show, in which three teams of two prove seek to progress along a board game-style path by answering questions. Each duo are allowed to determine how many spaces they would like to move by answering the corresponding number of questions. Landing on a space reveals it's hidden contents, which could amount to a prize, or a 'hot spot', which causes contestants to forfeit their gathered winnings.(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong presents the general knowledge quiz as famous faces from children's television programmes take part. This edition features CBBC presenters Anthea Turner and Andy Crane, CBeebies stars Justin Fletcher and Gemma Hunt, children's TV hosts Dave Benson-Phillips and Gareth Jones, and former presenters Sally James and Geoffrey Hayes. The famous faces try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. Co-host Richard Osman has all the answers.(n)

programme.title: Strike It Lucky
programme.desc: Michael Barrymore hosts the madcap game show, in which three teams of two prove seek to progress along a board game-style path by answering questions. Each duo are allowed to determine how many spaces they would like to move by answering the corresponding number of questions. Landing on a space reveals it's hidden contents, which could amount to a prize, or a 'hot spot', which causes contestants to forfeit their gathered winnings.(n)
